target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 focuses on resolving ISS-001 (path trace reconstruction) and ISS-002 (ingestion backpressure). Path trace reconstruction is addressed by a ClickHouse materialized view pre-aggregating hop sequences by flow_id ordered on ingress_timestamp_ns, eliminating per-query full table scans. Ingestion backpressure is handled by an asyncio ring buffer with configurable high-water mark that applies backpressure to the gRPC stream reader when ClickHouse insert latency exceeds 200ms.

fix_report.action_taken: Added explicit path trace reconstruction design using ClickHouse materialized view path_traces_mv pre-aggregating telemetry_hops by flow_id ordered on ingress_timestamp_ns. Updated data_model with mv definition and workflows section with PathTraceReconstructor module logic.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Auditor can verify the path trace reconstruction algorithm is fully specified and query-efficient

fix_report.action_taken: Specified asyncio ring buffer (configurable size, default 500K records) with high-water mark at 80% capacity triggering gRPC read pause. ClickHouse insert retried up to 3 times with exponential backoff before raising ingestion_error alert. Updated workflows and system_components.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Ingestion backpressure strategy is documented and observable via Prometheus metrics

target_output.title: INT Telemetry Research Platform — P4 Switch Congestion Analysis API
target_output.executive_summary: A research platform providing API-first access to in-band network telemetry data collected from P4-programmable switches in a university testbed. Python FastAPI backend ingests INT records from the P4 collector gRPC stream at up to 500K records/second into a 3-node ClickHouse cluster. Path trace reconstruction uses a materialized view over per-hop INT records. REST API exposes per-flow latency histograms, queue depth snapshots, and reconstructed path traces for congestion control algorithm research. React 18 dashboard with D3.js visualizations for experiment management and live telemetry monitoring.
target_output.architecture_overview: FastAPI API tier with three routers (experiments, telemetry, nodes) backed by PostgreSQL 16 for relational experiment metadata and ClickHouse 3-node ReplicatedMergeTree cluster for INT telemetry time-series. A separate async INTIngestionWorker process consumes the P4 INT collector gRPC stream, buffers records in an asyncio ring buffer (default 500K record capacity), and batch-inserts to ClickHouse every 100ms or when buffer reaches 10K records. Path trace reconstruction is served via ClickHouse materialized view path_traces_mv pre-aggregating telemetry_hops ordered by ingress_timestamp_ns per flow_id, enabling sub-100ms path trace queries. React 18 SPA with D3.js serves experiment management and interactive telemetry visualization.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, asyncio, grpcio for P4 collector consumer, clickhouse-driver, SQLAlchemy 2, pydantic v2. Data: ClickHouse 3-node ReplicatedMergeTree cluster via ClickHouse Operator, PostgreSQL 16 via CloudNativePG, no external cache. Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, D3.js v7 for histograms and path topology rendering, React Query. Infra: Bare-metal Kubernetes, GitHub Actions, Prometheus, Grafana, Loki.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Experiment Lifecycle: register experiment with switch node filter set and flow filter rules, start ingestion, monitor via SSE live rate feed, teardown with automatic archival to NFS. Latency Analysis: per-flow p50/p95/p99 latency histogram query with configurable time window and flow filter. Path Trace Reconstruction: reconstruct per-flow hop sequence from INT records with per-hop queue depth and processing delay annotations. Queue Depth Timeline: time-series query of queue occupancy per switch port across experiment window. Congestion Event Log: detected congestion events filterable by switch, flow, and severity.
target_output.system_components: FastAPI API service (2 replicas, Kubernetes Deployment). INTIngestionWorker (1 replica per P4 collector gRPC stream, asyncio ring buffer with 500K record capacity, 80% high-water backpressure, 100ms flush interval). ClickHouse 3-node ReplicatedMergeTree cluster with Distributed table routing by consistent hash of flow_id; path_traces_mv materialized view on telemetry_hops. PostgreSQL 16 primary with streaming replica for experiment metadata. React 18 Nginx-served SPA (2 replicas). Prometheus and Grafana on cluster for metrics and dashboards. NFS mount for archived experiment datasets.
target_output.workflows: INT Ingestion with Backpressure: INTIngestionWorker opens gRPC stream to P4 collector. Records decoded to INT structs and appended to asyncio ring buffer. Batch consumer reads up to 10K records every 100ms and issues ClickHouse async insert. If ring buffer utilization exceeds 80%, gRPC stream read is paused until buffer drains below 60%, preventing OOM. On ClickHouse insert failure, retry with exponential backoff up to 3 attempts; if all fail, emit ingestion_error metric and Prometheus alert. Path Trace Query: Researcher queries GET /telemetry/path-traces?experiment_id=&flow_id=&from=&to=. FastAPI routes to ClickHouse query against path_traces_mv, selecting hop records for the flow ordered by ingress_timestamp_ns within the time window. Response serialized as array of hop objects with switch_id, ingress/egress port, queue_depth_bytes, and processing_delay_ns. Experiment Teardown: POST /experiments/{id}/teardown transitions experiment to teardown state. Background job exports telemetry_flows and congestion_events summary to NFS archive, generates JSON summary report with per-flow p95 latency, observed congestion event count, and peak queue depth, then marks experiment archived and drops ClickHouse partition for that experiment_id.
target_output.data_model: ClickHouse tables — telemetry_flows (ReplicatedMergeTree partitioned by toYYYYMM(first_seen): flow_id UInt64, experiment_id UUID, src_ip IPv6, dst_ip IPv6, src_port UInt16, dst_port UInt16, protocol UInt8, first_seen DateTime64 ns, last_seen DateTime64 ns). telemetry_hops (ReplicatedMergeTree partitioned by experiment_id: hop_id UInt64, flow_id UInt64, experiment_id UUID, switch_id UInt32, ingress_port UInt16, egress_port UInt16, queue_depth_bytes UInt32, ingress_timestamp_ns UInt64, processing_delay_ns UInt32). path_traces_mv (Materialized view on telemetry_hops: SELECT flow_id, experiment_id, groupArray of hop tuples ordered by ingress_timestamp_ns, min(ingress_timestamp_ns) AS trace_start GROUP BY flow_id, experiment_id). queue_snapshots (ReplicatedMergeTree: switch_id, experiment_id, port_id, captured_at_ns UInt64, queue_occupancy_bytes UInt32). PostgreSQL tables — experiments (id UUID PK, researcher_id FK, title, switch_node_ids INT[], flow_filters JSONB, status ENUM registered/active/teardown/archived). congestion_events (id UUID PK, experiment_id FK, switch_id, flow_id, queue_depth_bytes, detected_at, duration_ms). researchers (id UUID PK, username, email, role ENUM researcher/lab_admin).
target_output.api_design: REST API at /api/v1. POST /experiments — create experiment with switch_node_ids and flow_filters. PATCH /experiments/{id}/start — begin ingestion. POST /experiments/{id}/teardown — archive and clean up. GET /telemetry/flows?experiment_id= — list flows with record counts. GET /telemetry/flows/{flow_id}/latency-histogram?experiment_id=&from=&to=&percentiles=p50,p95,p99 — returns histogram buckets. GET /telemetry/path-traces?experiment_id=&flow_id=&from=&to= — returns ordered hop array from path_traces_mv. GET /telemetry/queue-depth?experiment_id=&switch_id=&port_id=&from=&to= — queue occupancy time-series. GET /congestion-events?experiment_id= — congestion event list. SSE /experiments/{id}/ingestion-rate — live records/second stream.
target_output.security_and_compliance: JWT authentication on all endpoints issued on username/password login; researcher and lab_admin roles enforced per endpoint. TLS 1.3 on all API and ClickHouse client connections. Per-researcher ClickHouse query quota: max 2 concurrent queries, max 10 GB scan per query enforced via ClickHouse user profiles. API rate limit: 30 requests/minute per JWT on /telemetry endpoints to protect ClickHouse during active experiments.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Bare-metal Kubernetes with int-dev and int-prod namespaces. API: 2 replicas with Kubernetes readiness probe on /health. INTIngestionWorker: 1 replica, restart policy OnFailure. ClickHouse: 3-node ReplicatedMergeTree via ClickHouse Operator with ZooKeeper ensemble for coordination. PostgreSQL: CloudNativePG primary plus streaming replica. GitHub Actions CI: ruff, mypy, pytest (unit with synthetic INT packet fixtures, integration against int-dev ClickHouse), Docker build, kubectl apply to int-dev, manual promotion to int-prod.
target_output.observability: Prometheus metrics: int_records_ingested_total counter, int_ring_buffer_utilization gauge (triggers backpressure alert over 80%), int_batch_flush_duration_seconds histogram, clickhouse_insert_errors_total counter, api_query_duration_seconds histogram by endpoint. Grafana dashboards: Ingestion Health (records/sec, buffer utilization, insert error rate), Query Performance (p95 latency histogram query time, path trace query time), Experiment Status (active experiments, congestion event rate). Alert: int_ring_buffer_utilization over 90% for 2 min, clickhouse_insert_errors_total over 10 in 1 min.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero cloud cost — all on-premises lab servers. ClickHouse storage estimate: at 500K records/second, 100 bytes per hop record, 1-hour active experiment equals approximately 180 GB per experiment. Partition drop on experiment archival reclaims storage. NFS archive retains summary JSON only after 6 months. Ingestion worker single-threaded asyncio sufficient for current 500K rec/s; parallelism via multiple gRPC stream workers if rate exceeds 1M rec/s.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): P4 INT collector gRPC consumer, ClickHouse ingestion with backpressure, experiment lifecycle API, latency histogram and path trace endpoints, React dashboard with D3.js histogram view, Grafana ingestion metrics. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Queue depth timeline endpoint, congestion event detection and logging, experiment teardown archival with summary report, path trace topology visualization in React. Phase 3 (4 weeks): WebSocket streaming for live INT record visualization during active experiments, ClickHouse query result caching for repeated histogram queries, ML anomaly detection on queue depth time-series.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 strict mypy. ruff for linting. pytest with synthetic INT packet binary fixtures for ingestion worker unit tests and ClickHouse insert integration tests. API endpoint tests use testcontainers for ClickHouse and PostgreSQL. 80% coverage gate. ClickHouse schema migrations via Flyway-compatible migration scripts applied in CI. Path trace materialized view changes require experiment data validation before prod promotion.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: ClickHouse node failure during active experiment loses unwritten buffer. Mitigation: ReplicatedMergeTree with factor 2 ensures no data loss; ring buffer WAL not implemented at MVP — accepted risk for research prototype. Risk: path_traces_mv rebuild time on ClickHouse restart if MV state is lost. Mitigation: MV is incremental on insert; only records from restart gap are missing, acceptable for research workload. Tradeoff: No Redis cache — ClickHouse materialized view serves as query acceleration layer, simpler operational model for lab environment. Risk: Researcher query monopolizing ClickHouse during active ingestion. Mitigation: Per-user quota profiles enforced at ClickHouse server level.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed ClickHouse ReplicatedMergeTree over TimescaleDB for columnar compression advantage at INT record volume. Confirmed materialized view approach over on-query aggregation for path traces. Confirmed gRPC backpressure via stream read pause over dropping records as researcher data integrity is paramount.

metadata.notes: Round 2 research prototype for P4 INT telemetry; fixes path trace reconstruction design and ClickHouse backpressure handling.

profile.domain: LLM-Assisted TDM Circuit Inventory Migration

project_goal.value: Migrate legacy TDM circuit inventory records from the operator's aging OSS into a modern cloud-based resource management platform using LLM-assisted data extraction and entity resolution to reconcile inconsistent legacy schemas
project_goal.rationale: Legacy OSS data is unstructured and schema-inconsistent; manual migration estimated at 18 months; LLM pipeline targets 3-month completion

target_users.value: Network planning engineers and provisioning team leads who validate migrated circuit records and approve entity resolution merges
target_users.rationale: Primary stakeholders for data quality outcomes identified in discovery

project_class.rationale: LLM-driven data extraction and embedding-based entity resolution are the core differentiating capabilities

capabilities.rationale: LLM pipeline for extraction, batch processing of legacy records, OSS SOAP API integration, and internal reviewer dashboard

complexity_level.rationale: Millions of legacy circuit records, LLM extraction pipeline with human review, entity resolution clustering, and cross-system validation

risk_level.rationale: Incorrect migration of circuit records could cause service disruptions; confidential operator infrastructure data involved

data_sensitivity.rationale: TDM circuit inventory contains network element identifiers, trunk group capacities, and interconnect details classified as confidential by the operator

external_exposure.rationale: Migration system accessible only from operator internal network; no external APIs exposed

access_model.value: Azure AD SSO with migration_engineer and migration_admin roles; only migration_admin may commit approved merges to the target database
access_model.rationale: Audit trail requires named approver for every entity resolution commit

feature_scope.value: Legacy OSS batch extraction via SOAP APIs, LLM-driven field normalization and schema mapping, embedding-based entity resolution with human review dashboard, bulk migration commit to target system, post-migration validation against live topology, reconciliation exception tracking
feature_scope.rationale: Complete lifecycle from raw legacy records to validated target inventory

mvp_scope.value: Batch extraction from two legacy OSS sources, LLM normalization pipeline for TDM circuit free-text fields, entity resolution review dashboard, bulk commit to target PostgreSQL schema
mvp_scope.rationale: Covers the two highest-priority OSS sources representing 70% of circuit inventory

security_baseline.value: Azure AD SSO for all access, AKS pod identity for Azure OpenAI API calls, all circuit data encrypted at rest via Azure-managed keys, audit log of every LLM extraction and entity resolution decision
security_baseline.rationale: Operator data governance and internal audit requirements

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with TypeScript for the entity resolution review dashboard and migration progress monitoring
frontend_stack.rationale: Team Next.js expertise; server components for large entity cluster table rendering

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI for control API; Celery workers on AKS for batch extraction and LLM pipeline jobs
backend_stack.rationale: Python LLM ecosystem (LangChain, sentence-transformers); FastAPI for async control endpoints

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server for migration state, entity resolution records, and target schema; Redis 7 for Celery broker
data_platform.rationale: Relational model for structured migration audit trail; Azure managed service for operational simplicity

hosting_target.value: AKS (Azure Kubernetes Service) in the operator's Azure tenant
hosting_target.rationale: Operator has existing AKS infrastructure and Azure AD integration

privacy_retention_policy.value: Raw legacy extracts retained 6 months post-migration for rollback; migration audit log retained 5 years per data governance policy
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Operator data governance policy requires full audit trail of migration decisions

future_scope.value: Additional legacy OSS sources, automated post-migration reconciliation scheduling, LLM confidence score threshold tuning dashboard
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 scope after MVP validation

constraints.value: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o rate limit: 50K tokens/minute; migration must complete within 3-month window; no direct database access to legacy OSS — SOAP API only
constraints.rationale: Rate limit and SOAP-only access are hard infrastructure constraints

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor for AKS metrics, structured logging to Log Analytics workspace, Celery task monitoring via Flower dashboard, migration progress tracked in PostgreSQL migration_batches table
observability_baseline.rationale: Operator standard Azure observability stack

execution_preference.value: Two-week sprints with milestone gates aligned to migration batch completion checkpoints
execution_preference.rationale: Migration timeline requires predictable milestone tracking

compliance_context.value: Operator internal data governance policy requires audit log of all automated data transformation decisions; LLM extraction outputs must be human-reviewable before commit to target system
compliance_context.rationale: Confidential circuit data subject to internal governance; LLM decisions not auto-committed without review

llm_integration.value: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o for free-text field normalization and schema mapping; text-embedding-3-large for entity resolution embeddings; structured output mode for deterministic JSON extraction
llm_integration.rationale: Azure OpenAI within operator Azure tenant satisfies data residency requirements; no data leaves the operator's environment

project.goal: LLM-assisted migration of TDM circuit inventory from legacy OSS to modern resource platform
project.target_users: Network planning engineers and provisioning team leads
project.timeline: 3-month migration window

backend.framework: FastAPI control API, Celery batch workers
backend.llm: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o via LangChain structured output, text-embedding-3-large for entity resolution

security.auth: Azure AD SSO with MSAL, migration_engineer and migration_admin roles
security.transport: TLS 1.3, AKS pod identity for Azure OpenAI API authentication

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database Flexible Server

constraints.llm_rate: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o 50K tokens/minute rate limit
constraints.legacy_access: SOAP API only to legacy OSS

confirmed_decisions.llm: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o within operator tenant for data residency

reasoner_reviews.product: The human review gate before entity resolution commits is the right governance model — fully automated LLM commits to circuit inventory would be unacceptable given the operational risk of incorrect circuit records. The reviewer dashboard must surface LLM confidence scores alongside the raw legacy field values so reviewers can make informed decisions quickly.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The critical throughput constraint is the Azure OpenAI 50K tokens/minute rate limit. With an average TDM circuit record at approximately 500 tokens for normalization, the pipeline can process approximately 100 records/minute per batch worker. For a 5M record migration in 3 months, the system needs approximately 12 parallel workers running continuously. Token budget allocation between extraction and embedding generation must be explicitly designed. The entity resolution clustering step using cosine similarity on embeddings should run after batch extraction is complete to avoid re-embedding overhead.
reasoner_reviews.security: AKS workload identity for Azure OpenAI API calls ensures no static API keys exist in the codebase. Audit log of every LLM extraction decision with the raw input, model output, and confidence score provides the paper trail required by the data governance policy. Restrict network policy on AKS so only the LLM pipeline pods can reach the Azure OpenAI endpoint.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 3-month migration window requires daily progress tracking against the total circuit count. Recommend a migration_batches table tracking batch status, record counts, and LLM token consumption per batch to enable timeline forecasting.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 1 plan did not specify how the LLM token rate limit is managed across parallel Celery workers — without a shared token budget controller, workers will hit rate limits and fail. Also missing: the embedding-based clustering algorithm parameters (similarity threshold for merge candidates).

backend.service_design: FastAPI control API with routers: /migration-batches, /entity-resolutions, /validation-jobs. Celery workers: extract_batch (SOAP pull + LLM normalize), generate_embeddings (text-embedding-3-large per entity), cluster_entities (cosine similarity clustering), validate_post_migration (topology cross-reference).
backend.llm_integration_design: LangChain structured output with GPT-4o and Pydantic response model TDMCircuitSchema for deterministic JSON extraction. Token budget managed by Redis-backed rate limiter (TokenBucketLimiter) refilling at 50K tokens/minute shared across all workers. Embeddings generated in 100-record batches via Azure OpenAI embeddings endpoint. Entity resolution: cosine similarity threshold 0.92 for merge candidates, 0.95 for auto-cluster (still human-reviewed).
backend.background_jobs: extract_batch: pulls 500 SOAP records, sends to GPT-4o with TDMCircuitSchema prompt, stores normalized records in legacy_inventory_records table with extraction_confidence score. generate_embeddings: runs after batch extraction, generates text-embedding-3-large embeddings for circuit description fields. cluster_entities: DBSCAN on embedding vectors with epsilon=0.08 (cosine distance), groups merge candidates into entity_resolution_clusters. validate_post_migration: cross-references migrated circuit IDs against live network topology API.
backend.failure_handling: Token rate limit exceeded: Celery task retries with exponential backoff using Redis-backed backoff key per worker. SOAP API timeout: retry 3x then mark batch as extraction_failed and alert. LLM structured output parsing failure: fallback to unstructured extraction with low_confidence flag and route to manual review queue.

security.auth_design: Azure AD MSAL with migration_engineer (read, submit review decisions) and migration_admin (approve merges, commit to target) roles. FastAPI dependency validates Azure AD JWT and checks role claims.
security.secrets_management: AKS workload identity bound to Azure OpenAI and Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server — no static connection strings or API keys in pods. Key Vault for SOAP API endpoint credentials.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: migration_audit_log table records every LLM extraction event with batch_id, record_id, model_id, input_tokens, output_tokens, confidence_score, raw_legacy_value, normalized_value, operator_id if human-reviewed, timestamp. Append-only enforced via PostgreSQL trigger.

data.entities: legacy_inventory_records (id UUID PK, batch_id FK, source_oss, raw_soap_xml TEXT, extracted_at, extraction_confidence FLOAT, normalized_circuit_json JSONB, review_status ENUM pending/approved/rejected/auto_merged). migration_batches (id UUID PK, source_oss, total_records INT, extracted_count, normalized_count, merged_count, committed_count, status ENUM running/paused/complete/failed, started_at, completed_at, token_usage_total BIGINT). entity_resolutions (id UUID PK, cluster_id, record_ids UUID[], similarity_score FLOAT, resolution_action ENUM merge/keep_separate/manual_review, reviewed_by, reviewed_at, target_resource_id FK). network_elements (id UUID PK, element_type, location, vendor, capacity_ds0 INT). tdm_circuits (id UUID PK, circuit_id_canonical, network_element_a FK, network_element_z FK, trunk_group_id, capacity_ds0, service_type ENUM voice/data/interconnect, status, migrated_from_oss, migration_batch_id FK). reconciliation_exceptions (id UUID PK, circuit_id FK, topology_source, discrepancy_type, detected_at, resolved BOOLEAN).
data.migration_notes: SOAP extraction parallelized across 12 Celery workers with shared token budget. Entity resolution clustering run as offline batch after all records in a batch are extracted and embedded. Target schema commits are transactional — all circuits in a resolved cluster committed atomically or rolled back.

devops.deployment_topology: AKS with three namespaces: migration-dev, migration-staging, migration-prod. Deployments: api (2 replicas), celery-worker (12 replicas for LLM pipeline, KEDA scaled on Redis queue depth), celery-beat (1 replica). Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server. Azure Cache for Redis.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps pipeline: mypy, ruff, pytest (unit tests with mocked Azure OpenAI responses, integration tests against migration-dev), Docker build, Helm chart deploy via ArgoCD to migration-staging then manual gate to migration-prod.
devops.cost_controls: Azure OpenAI token consumption tracked per batch in migration_batches.token_usage_total. Alert when daily token spend projects to exceed monthly budget. KEDA scales workers down to 2 replicas during off-hours when no active migration batch is running.

previous_plan.title: TDM Circuit Inventory Migration System v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Python FastAPI backend with 12 Celery workers processing legacy OSS SOAP extracts through Azure OpenAI GPT-4o normalization pipeline. Entity resolution via embedding similarity. Next.js reviewer dashboard.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: FastAPI control API, Celery workers for SOAP extraction and LLM pipeline, PostgreSQL for migration state, Next.js for reviewer dashboard. Azure OpenAI for extraction and embeddings.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 must resolve ISS-001 (token rate limit coordination across 12 workers) and ISS-002 (entity resolution clustering parameters). The token bucket pattern with Redis as the shared counter is the standard solution — workers acquire tokens before dispatching to Azure OpenAI and block/retry if insufficient. DBSCAN with cosine distance epsilon=0.08 and similarity confidence tiers provides the defined merge/manual-review split that reviewers need to trust the system.

fix_report.action_taken: Added Redis-backed TokenBucketLimiter class shared across all Celery workers; workers acquire token budget before each Azure OpenAI call; if bucket has insufficient tokens, worker sleeps with jitter backoff until next refill cycle (1 second). Updated system_components, workflows, and backend specialist subplan.
fix_report.expected_outcome: No Azure OpenAI 429 errors under sustained 12-worker parallel extraction; token consumption predictable

fix_report.action_taken: Specified DBSCAN clustering with cosine distance metric, epsilon=0.08, min_samples=2. Defined confidence tiers: similarity over 0.95 routes to merge_candidate queue (still human-reviewed), 0.92-0.95 routes to manual_review queue. Updated data_model entity_resolutions and workflows.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Entity resolution merge candidates have defined quality thresholds; reviewers see confidence tier labels in dashboard

target_output.title: LLM-Assisted TDM Circuit Inventory Migration Platform
target_output.executive_summary: An AI-driven batch migration system that extracts TDM circuit records from two legacy OSS systems via SOAP APIs, normalizes free-text fields using Azure OpenAI GPT-4o structured output, and clusters duplicate records using text-embedding-3-large cosine similarity. Token consumption managed by a Redis-backed shared token bucket across 12 Celery workers. A Next.js reviewer dashboard surfaces entity resolution clusters with confidence tiers for human approval before atomic commits to the target PostgreSQL schema. Runs on AKS within the operator's Azure tenant for data residency compliance.
target_output.architecture_overview: FastAPI control API backed by four Celery pipeline stages: extract_batch (SOAP pull), normalize_with_llm (GPT-4o structured extraction), generate_embeddings (text-embedding-3-large), and cluster_entities (DBSCAN, epsilon=0.08 cosine distance). All Azure OpenAI calls gated by a Redis-backed TokenBucketLimiter (capacity 50K tokens/minute, shared across all workers) that blocks workers with jitter backoff when the bucket is depleted. PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Flexible Server stores migration state, entity resolution records, and target circuit schema. Next.js 14 reviewer dashboard with server components for high-volume entity cluster rendering. AKS workload identity authenticates pods to Azure OpenAI and PostgreSQL without static credentials.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, Celery 5, LangChain 0.2 with structured output, azure-ai-openai SDK, scikit-learn DBSCAN for clustering, SQLAlchemy 2, pydantic v2. Data: PostgreSQL 16 Azure Flexible Server, Redis 7 Azure Cache (Celery broker and token bucket). Frontend: Next.js 14 App Router, TypeScript, React Query. Auth: Azure AD MSAL. Infra: AKS, Azure DevOps, Azure Monitor, Log Analytics. LLM: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o (extraction), text-embedding-3-large (entity embeddings).
target_output.functional_feature_map: Batch Extraction Pipeline: SOAP record pull from legacy OSS, GPT-4o normalization with TDMCircuitSchema structured output, extraction confidence scoring. Entity Resolution: text-embedding-3-large embeddings, DBSCAN clustering with epsilon=0.08, confidence tier assignment (merge_candidate over 0.95, manual_review 0.92-0.95). Reviewer Dashboard: paginated entity cluster review with side-by-side legacy vs normalized field comparison, confidence score display, approve/reject/keep_separate actions. Migration Commit: atomic transactional commit of approved clusters to target tdm_circuits and network_elements schema. Post-Migration Validation: cross-reference migrated circuits against live topology API, flag reconciliation exceptions. Token Budget Monitoring: per-batch Azure OpenAI token usage tracking with daily projection alerts.
target_output.system_components: FastAPI API service (2 replicas). Celery Worker pool (12 replicas, KEDA ScaledObject on Redis queue depth). Redis-backed TokenBucketLimiter: single Redis sorted set token_bucket:openai with atomic INCR/EXPIRE pattern, capacity 50K tokens/minute refilled every second at 833 tokens. Celery Beat (1 replica). PostgreSQL 16 Azure Flexible Server with zone-redundant HA. Redis 7 Azure Cache for Celery broker and token bucket. AKS Workload Identity bound to Azure OpenAI and Key Vault. Next.js 14 frontend service. Azure DevOps for CI/CD. Azure Monitor + Log Analytics for observability.
target_output.workflows: LLM Extraction with Token Budget: Celery extract_batch worker pulls 500 SOAP records from legacy OSS. For each record, worker calls TokenBucketLimiter.acquire(estimated_tokens) — if bucket has sufficient capacity, proceeds with GPT-4o call using LangChain structured output returning TDMCircuitSchema JSON; if insufficient, sleeps with random jitter (0.1-0.5s) and retries until tokens available. Extracted records stored in legacy_inventory_records with extraction_confidence. Token usage logged to migration_batches.token_usage_total. Entity Resolution Clustering: After batch extraction completes, generate_embeddings task batches 100 records and calls text-embedding-3-large API (also token-budgeted). cluster_entities task loads embeddings, runs DBSCAN with cosine distance metric, epsilon=0.08, min_samples=2. Clusters with max pairwise similarity over 0.95 assigned confidence_tier merge_candidate; 0.92-0.95 assigned manual_review. Results stored in entity_resolutions. Reviewer approval triggers atomic PostgreSQL transaction committing all circuits in cluster to target schema or rolling back entirely. Post-Migration Validation: validate_post_migration Celery task iterates migrated tdm_circuits, queries live topology API for each circuit_id_canonical, writes reconciliation_exceptions for any circuit absent from topology.
target_output.data_model: legacy_inventory_records (id UUID PK, batch_id FK, source_oss VARCHAR, raw_soap_xml TEXT, extracted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, extraction_confidence FLOAT, normalized_circuit_json JSONB, review_status ENUM pending/approved/rejected). migration_batches (id UUID PK, source_oss, total_records INT, extracted_count INT, merged_count INT, committed_count INT, status ENUM running/paused/complete/failed, token_usage_total BIGINT, started_at, completed_at). entity_resolutions (id UUID PK, cluster_id UUID, record_ids UUID[], similarity_score FLOAT, confidence_tier ENUM merge_candidate/manual_review, resolution_action ENUM merge/keep_separate/manual_review, reviewed_by, reviewed_at, target_resource_id UUID FK). tdm_circuits (id UUID PK, circuit_id_canonical VARCHAR UNIQUE, network_element_a UUID FK, network_element_z UUID FK, trunk_group_id, capacity_ds0 INT, service_type ENUM voice/data/interconnect, status ENUM active/inactive/decommissioned, migration_batch_id FK). network_elements (id UUID PK, element_type, location, vendor, capacity_ds0 INT). reconciliation_exceptions (id UUID PK, circuit_id UUID FK, topology_source, discrepancy_type, detected_at, resolved BOOLEAN). migration_audit_log (id UUID PK, batch_id, record_id, model_id, input_tokens INT, output_tokens INT, confidence_score FLOAT, raw_legacy_value TEXT, normalized_value TEXT, operator_id, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ) — append-only via PostgreSQL trigger.
target_output.api_design: REST API at /api/v1. POST /migration-batches — initiate batch with source_oss and record_filter. GET /migration-batches/{id} — status, progress counters, token_usage_total. GET /entity-resolutions?status=pending&confidence_tier=manual_review — paginated review queue. PATCH /entity-resolutions/{id}/decide — body {action: merge|keep_separate|reject}, migration_admin only. POST /migration-batches/{id}/commit — trigger atomic target schema commit for all approved resolutions in batch, migration_admin only. GET /reconciliation-exceptions?batch_id= — post-migration validation results. GET /migration-audit-log?batch_id=&from=&to= — LLM decision audit export.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD SSO via MSAL; migration_engineer role for read and review decisions; migration_admin for commit and batch initiation. AKS workload identity for Azure OpenAI and PostgreSQL — no static credentials. Key Vault for SOAP API endpoint secrets. All circuit data encrypted at rest via Azure-managed keys on Flexible Server. migration_audit_log append-only via PostgreSQL row-level trigger (insert only, no updates or deletes). Network policy on AKS restricts Azure OpenAI endpoint access to LLM pipeline pods only. Data governance: LLM outputs not auto-committed without human review per compliance_context requirement.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: AKS with migration-dev, migration-staging, migration-prod namespaces. Deployments: api (2 replicas), celery-worker (12 replicas, KEDA ScaledObject watching Redis llen migration_queue, scale to 2 during off-hours). PostgreSQL Azure Flexible Server with zone-redundant HA standby. Redis Azure Cache for Celery broker and token bucket. Azure DevOps pipeline: mypy, ruff, pytest (mocked Azure OpenAI unit tests, integration tests against migration-dev namespace), Docker build and push to ACR, Helm chart deploy via ArgoCD. Manual promotion gate from staging to prod namespace.
target_output.observability: Azure Monitor metrics for AKS node CPU and memory. Custom Prometheus metrics: token_bucket_utilization gauge (percent of 50K/min consumed), celery_tasks_failed_total by task_name, extraction_confidence_mean gauge, entity_resolution_pending_count gauge. Flower dashboard for Celery worker visibility. Log Analytics workspace for structured Python logs with batch_id and record_id correlation. Alert: token_bucket_utilization over 90% sustained 5 min, celery_tasks_failed_total over 20 in 10 min, extraction_confidence_mean below 0.75 (LLM output quality degradation).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o pricing: at 500 tokens/record average, 5M records = 2.5B tokens total across extraction and embedding — approximately $7,500 at current GPT-4o input pricing. KEDA scales workers to 2 replicas off-hours. AKS node pool with auto-scaler for burst capacity during peak extraction. PostgreSQL Flexible Server Burstable tier for dev, General Purpose for prod.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): SOAP extraction from two priority OSS sources, GPT-4o normalization pipeline with TokenBucketLimiter, PostgreSQL migration state schema, basic Next.js reviewer dashboard with approve/reject actions, Azure DevOps CI/CD. Phase 2 (4 weeks): text-embedding-3-large entity resolution with DBSCAN clustering, confidence tier display in dashboard, atomic target schema commit workflow, token usage forecasting. Phase 3 (4 weeks): post-migration validation against live topology API, reconciliation exception dashboard, additional OSS source integrations, LLM confidence threshold tuning interface.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 strict mypy. ruff for linting. LangChain structured output must have Pydantic model tests with representative legacy SOAP XML fixtures. TokenBucketLimiter must have unit tests for concurrent access simulation. 85% test coverage gate. All Azure OpenAI calls mocked in unit tests via respx. ADR required for DBSCAN parameter choices. Migration schema changes require migration_admin approval before prod deployment.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GPT-4o structured output fails on highly malformed legacy SOAP records. Mitigation: fallback prompt with unstructured extraction and low_confidence flag routes to manual queue. Risk: DBSCAN epsilon tuning — too tight misses real duplicates, too loose creates false merges. Mitigation: epsilon=0.08 calibrated on 1K-record sample set; tuning dashboard planned for Phase 3. Tradeoff: human review gate slows migration velocity but is required by data governance policy. Risk: Azure OpenAI model version changes affecting extraction consistency mid-migration. Mitigation: pin to specific GPT-4o API version in deployment config.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Azure OpenAI within operator tenant over third-party LLM APIs for data residency. Confirmed DBSCAN over hierarchical clustering for scalability at 5M record embedding set. Confirmed atomic transactional commit per cluster over record-by-record commits for rollback safety.

metadata.notes: Round 2 AI migration system; resolves token rate limit coordination and DBSCAN clustering parameter specification.

profile.domain: Telecom Stranded Asset Decommission Management

project_goal.value: Enable field technicians and planning engineers to identify, verify, and approve the decommission of stranded network assets — unused DSL ports, dark fiber segments, and redundant DSLAM cards — to reduce operational and leasing costs
project_goal.rationale: Manual spreadsheet tracking of stranded assets is unreliable and prevents systematic cost reduction

target_users.value: Field technicians submitting site survey findings and planning engineers reviewing decommission candidates with OSS utilization data
target_users.rationale: Two primary user roles with distinct workflows identified in discovery

project_class.rationale: Requires field technician mobile-friendly web forms, planning engineer dashboard, and admin panel for workflow configuration

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, admin_panel, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Full web application with role-based access, analytics for cost savings tracking, and admin configuration panel

complexity_level.rationale: Standard CRUD workflows with OSS integration and cost calculation logic; no real-time or AI complexity

risk_level.rationale: Incorrect decommission approvals could cause service disruption; internal data only

data_sensitivity.rationale: Network asset inventory and utilization data classified as internal operational information

external_exposure.rationale: Accessible only from operator internal network; field technicians connect via VPN

access_model.value: Keycloak RBAC with field_technician, planning_engineer, and admin roles; only planning_engineer may approve decommission orders
access_model.rationale: Approval authority must be restricted to planning engineers to prevent unauthorized decommissions

feature_scope.value: Site survey submission with idle asset marking, OSS utilization data cross-reference, decommission candidate review and approval workflow, cost savings projection calculation, asset register integration, admin panel for workflow configuration and cost model parameters
feature_scope.rationale: Complete workflow from field survey to approved decommission order with cost tracking

mvp_scope.value: Field technician survey submission, planning engineer review dashboard with manual utilization lookup, decommission order approval, basic cost savings calculation
mvp_scope.rationale: Manual OSS lookup acceptable for MVP; automated integration in Phase 2

security_baseline.value: Keycloak SSO for all access, TLS 1.3 in transit, RBAC enforcement for decommission approval actions, audit log for all approval decisions
security_baseline.rationale: Internal system with medium risk; SSO and RBAC are required minimum controls

frontend_stack.value: Vue 3 with TypeScript; Tailwind CSS for responsive mobile-friendly layout for field technicians
frontend_stack.rationale: Team Vue expertise; mobile-responsive design important for field technician use on tablets

backend_stack.value: Node.js 20 with Express; RESTful API
backend_stack.rationale: Team JavaScript expertise; simple CRUD workload does not require specialized framework

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on GCP Cloud SQL
data_platform.rationale: Managed relational database with minimal operational overhead on GCP

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run for API and frontend serving
hosting_target.rationale: Serverless containers reduce operational overhead for this internal tool

privacy_retention_policy.value: Decommission order records retained 5 years for operational audit; survey submissions retained 2 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Operational audit requirements for network change records

future_scope.value: Automated OSS utilization data pull integration, GIS map view for asset locations, bulk decommission order generation
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 and 3 roadmap items post-MVP validation

constraints.value: No cache layer; GCP Cloud SQL budget under $200/month; field technicians access via VPN on tablets
constraints.rationale: Simple tool does not justify cache infrastructure; tablet UX requirement

observability_baseline.value: Grafana Cloud with Prometheus for Cloud Run metrics, structured JSON logging via GCP Cloud Logging, alert on failed decommission order submissions
observability_baseline.rationale: Standard GCP observability setup for internal tools

execution_preference.value: Two-week sprints with internal stakeholder demos
execution_preference.rationale: Iterative delivery with regular planning engineer feedback

project.goal: Manage stranded network asset decommission workflow end-to-end
project.timeline: MVP in Q2 for cost reduction program

frontend.state: Pinia for local state, fetch API for server state

devops.environments: dev, staging, production Cloud Run services

constraints.access: Internal network only, VPN for field technicians

confirmed_decisions.oss_integration: Manual lookup in MVP, automated pull in Phase 2
confirmed_decisions.hosting: GCP Cloud Run for serverless simplicity

reasoner_reviews.product: The dual workflow — field technician survey submission followed by planning engineer review with OSS utilization cross-reference — is the core value delivery. The cost savings projection on each decommission candidate is what converts planning engineers to active users. The projection formula (DSL port monthly lease cost + power consumption cost per DSLAM card) must be configurable by admin to remain accurate as rates change.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Node.js Express with Prisma on Cloud Run is well-matched to this moderate-complexity CRUD application. No cache is appropriate given the constraint and usage pattern — planning engineers query a bounded set of pending decommission candidates, not a high-volume read workload. Cloud SQL with connection pooling via Prisma Accelerate or PgBouncer sidecar on Cloud Run is necessary to prevent connection exhaustion under Cloud Run scale-to-zero cold start behavior.
reasoner_reviews.security: Keycloak OIDC is the right SSO solution for this internal tool. The key security control beyond auth is the audit log for every approval decision — planning engineers must be accountable for each decommission order approval given the potential for service disruption. An append-only audit_decisions table satisfies this requirement without complex event sourcing.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Cloud SQL db-g1-small is sufficient for MVP at internal tool scale. Verify connection limit (25 for f1-micro) against Cloud Run max concurrency. Recommend db-g1-small at $50/month for headroom.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 plan still had thin content in phased_implementation — Phase 1 listed generic MVP items without specifying which decommission workflow steps are included. Cost savings calculation formula was referenced but not defined. These are the remaining weaknesses to address in Round 3.

backend.service_design: Express application with four route modules: /assets (network asset CRUD), /surveys (field technician submissions), /decommission-orders (review and approval workflow), /admin (cost model configuration). Prisma ORM for type-safe PostgreSQL access.
backend.business_modules: AssetCatalog: manages network_assets master registry including DSL ports, fiber segments, and DSLAM cards. SurveyProcessor: accepts field technician survey submissions, validates asset IDs against AssetCatalog, creates field_surveys and associated asset_utilization_records. DecommissionWorkflow: state machine (candidate -> under_review -> approved/rejected -> provisioning_notified -> decommissioned). CostCalculator: computes monthly_savings_estimate from asset type cost rates stored in cost_model_parameters table.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful JSON API. POST /surveys — submit site survey with array of idle asset observations. GET /decommission-orders?status=under_review — planning engineer review queue. PATCH /decommission-orders/{id}/approve — requires planning_engineer role, updates status and writes audit_decisions entry. GET /analytics/cost-savings — aggregate cost savings by month, asset type, and site.
backend.failure_handling: Prisma connection retry on Cloud Run cold start. Input validation via express-validator on all POST bodies. Decommission order status transitions validated against allowed state machine graph before database write.

security.auth_design: Keycloak OIDC with express-openid-connect middleware. JWT validated on every request. field_technician role: POST /surveys, GET /assets. planning_engineer role: GET/PATCH /decommission-orders, GET /analytics. admin role: all /admin endpoints.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: audit_decisions table records every approval or rejection with decommission_order_id, decision_maker_id, role, decision ENUM approved/rejected, rationale_text, timestamp. Append-only enforced via application layer — no UPDATE or DELETE operations on audit_decisions. Cloud Logging captures structured JSON request logs with user_id and order_id correlation.

data.entities: network_assets (id UUID PK, asset_type ENUM dsl_port/fiber_segment/dslam_card, site_id FK, location_code, vendor, capacity, status ENUM active/idle_candidate/under_review/decommissioned, last_active_date, created_at). sites (id UUID PK, site_name, region, address, site_manager_id). field_surveys (id UUID PK, technician_id FK, site_id FK, submitted_at, notes, status ENUM submitted/under_review/closed). asset_utilization_records (id UUID PK, survey_id FK, asset_id FK, observed_status ENUM idle/active/physically_absent, technician_notes). decommission_orders (id UUID PK, asset_id FK, survey_id FK, status ENUM candidate/under_review/approved/rejected/provisioning_notified/decommissioned, assigned_engineer_id, monthly_savings_estimate DECIMAL, approved_at, created_at). cost_savings_records (id UUID PK, decommission_order_id FK, asset_type, monthly_lease_saving DECIMAL, monthly_power_saving DECIMAL, recorded_at). cost_model_parameters (id UUID PK, asset_type, monthly_lease_cost DECIMAL, monthly_power_cost_kw DECIMAL, effective_from DATE). audit_decisions (id UUID PK, decommission_order_id FK, decision_maker_id, role, decision ENUM approved/rejected, rationale_text, timestamp).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL db-g1-small with private IP. Prisma ORM migrations for schema versioning. No cache — query volume bounded by internal user count.
data.analytics_events: cost_savings_records populated on each decommission order approval, enabling monthly cost savings aggregation by asset type and region without recomputation.

devops.deployment_topology: GCP Cloud Run services: decommission-api (min 0, max 10 instances, 1 vCPU / 512 MB) and decommission-frontend (Nginx serving Vue 3 SPA, min 1 instance). Cloud SQL proxy sidecar on API Cloud Run service for PostgreSQL connectivity. Keycloak deployed on GKE small cluster in same VPC.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ESLint, TypeScript tsc check, Jest unit tests for CostCalculator and DecommissionWorkflow state machine, Docker build, Cloud Run deploy via gcloud run deploy. Prisma migrations applied via Cloud Run job on deploy.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus via Cloud Run metrics exported to Grafana Cloud. Cloud Logging for structured JSON logs. Alerts: decommission order approval failure rate over 5% in 1 hour, Cloud Run cold start p99 over 3s.

frontend.app_structure: Vue 3 SPA with Vue Router. Three sections: Field Survey (mobile-optimized asset marking form), Planning Dashboard (decommission candidate review table with utilization data and cost projection), Admin Panel (cost model parameter management).
frontend.pages_and_flows: SurveySubmissionForm: mobile-responsive form for site visit recording; technician selects site, marks each asset as idle/active/absent, adds notes, submits. DecommissionReviewTable: paginated list of under_review orders with asset type, location, OSS utilization notes, monthly_savings_estimate, and approve/reject action. CostSavingsDashboard: bar chart of cumulative monthly savings by asset type over trailing 12 months.
frontend.state_management: Pinia stores for current user session and survey form draft state. Fetch API calls wrapped in composables with loading and error states.

target_output.title: Stranded Network Asset Decommission Management Platform
target_output.executive_summary: A mobile-responsive fullstack web application enabling field technicians to submit site survey findings on idle DSL ports, dark fiber segments, and DSLAM cards, and planning engineers to review decommission candidates with OSS utilization context and calculated cost savings projections. Built with Vue 3 on GCP Cloud Run backed by Node.js Express and PostgreSQL on Cloud SQL. Keycloak OIDC provides RBAC with field_technician and planning_engineer role separation. Cost savings projections calculated from configurable cost model parameters including monthly lease rates and power consumption figures by asset type.
target_output.architecture_overview: Vue 3 TypeScript SPA served from Cloud Run (Nginx) communicates over HTTPS with an Express REST API on Cloud Run backed by PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL via Prisma ORM with Cloud SQL Proxy sidecar. Keycloak OIDC validates JWTs on every API request. Four backend route modules handle asset catalog, survey submissions, decommission order workflow, and admin configuration. CostCalculator module computes monthly_savings_estimate at survey submission time from cost_model_parameters. No cache layer — bounded internal user count does not justify it. GitHub Actions CI/CD deploys to Cloud Run with Prisma migration jobs run as Cloud Run one-off tasks.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Vue Router 4, Pinia, D3.js for cost savings bar chart. Backend: Node.js 20, Express 4, Prisma ORM, express-openid-connect for Keycloak JWT validation, express-validator. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL db-g1-small with private IP. Auth: Keycloak 23 on GKE micro cluster. Infra: GCP Cloud Run, Cloud SQL Proxy, Cloud Logging, GitHub Actions. Observability: Grafana Cloud with Prometheus, Cloud Logging.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Field Survey Submission: mobile-optimized form for recording DSL port, fiber segment, and DSLAM card observations at a site visit; each asset marked idle/active/physically_absent with technician notes. Decommission Candidate Queue: auto-created from survey idle observations; displays asset_type, location, last_active_date, monthly_savings_estimate for planning engineer review. Decommission Approval Workflow: planning engineer reviews OSS utilization data, approves or rejects with rationale; approval triggers cost_savings_record creation. Cost Savings Dashboard: cumulative monthly savings bar chart by asset type over trailing 12 months. Admin Panel: edit cost_model_parameters (lease rates and power consumption by asset type, electricity rate per kWh). Audit Log: filterable history of all approval and rejection decisions by engineer and time period.
target_output.system_components: Cloud Run API service (Express, min 0, max 10 instances, Cloud SQL Proxy sidecar). Cloud Run Frontend service (Nginx serving Vue 3 SPA, min 1 instance). Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 db-g1-small with private IP and automated backups. Keycloak 23 on GKE micro node pool (2 nodes, n1-standard-1) with PostgreSQL backend. Cloud Logging for structured JSON logs. GitHub Actions for CI/CD.
target_output.workflows: Field Survey to Decommission Candidate: Field technician completes site visit, opens SurveySubmissionForm on tablet via VPN. Selects site from site_id dropdown, marks each asset in view as idle/active/physically_absent, adds notes. On submit, Express POST /surveys validates asset IDs, creates field_survey record and asset_utilization_records. For each asset_utilization_record with observed_status idle, CostCalculator computes monthly_savings_estimate = monthly_lease_cost[asset_type] + (power_consumption_kw[asset_type] * 730 * electricity_rate_per_kwh) from cost_model_parameters. decommission_order created with status candidate. Decommission Review and Approval: Planning engineer opens DecommissionReviewTable showing all under_review orders with asset type, location, OSS utilization notes, and monthly_savings_estimate. Approves or rejects with rationale text. On PATCH /decommission-orders/{id}/approve, Express validates planning_engineer role, transitions order to approved, writes audit_decisions entry and cost_savings_record.
target_output.data_model: network_assets (id UUID PK, asset_type ENUM dsl_port/fiber_segment/dslam_card, site_id UUID FK, location_code VARCHAR, vendor, capacity, status ENUM active/idle_candidate/under_review/approved/provisioning_notified/decommissioned, last_active_date DATE). sites (id UUID PK, site_name, region, address). field_surveys (id UUID PK, technician_id UUID, site_id UUID FK, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, notes TEXT, status ENUM submitted/under_review/closed). asset_utilization_records (id UUID PK, survey_id UUID FK, asset_id UUID FK, observed_status ENUM idle/active/physically_absent, technician_notes TEXT). decommission_orders (id UUID PK, asset_id UUID FK, survey_id UUID FK, status ENUM candidate/under_review/approved/rejected/provisioning_notified/decommissioned, assigned_engineer_id UUID, monthly_savings_estimate DECIMAL(10,2), approved_at, created_at). cost_savings_records (id UUID PK, decommission_order_id UUID FK, asset_type, monthly_lease_saving DECIMAL(10,2), monthly_power_saving DECIMAL(10,2), recorded_at). cost_model_parameters (id UUID PK, asset_type ENUM dsl_port/fiber_segment/dslam_card, monthly_lease_cost DECIMAL(10,2), power_consumption_kw DECIMAL(6,3), electricity_rate_per_kwh DECIMAL(6,4), effective_from DATE, created_by UUID). audit_decisions (id UUID PK, decommission_order_id UUID FK, decision_maker_id UUID, role VARCHAR, decision ENUM approved/rejected, rationale_text TEXT, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ). Formula: monthly_savings_estimate = monthly_lease_cost + (power_consumption_kw * 730 * electricity_rate_per_kwh).
target_output.api_design: REST API at /api/v1. POST /surveys — submit site survey with array of asset observations; returns created survey_id and list of created decommission_order IDs. GET /decommission-orders?status=under_review&asset_type=dsl_port — planning engineer review queue with pagination. PATCH /decommission-orders/{id}/approve — planning_engineer role required; body {rationale: string}. PATCH /decommission-orders/{id}/reject — planning_engineer role required; body {rationale: string}. GET /analytics/cost-savings?from=&to=&asset_type= — monthly savings aggregation. GET /assets?site_id=&status= — asset catalog with filters. GET PUT /admin/cost-model-parameters — admin role only. GET /audit-decisions?from=&to= — decision history.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Keycloak OIDC via express-openid-connect; JWT validated middleware on all routes. RBAC: field_technician (POST /surveys, GET /assets), planning_engineer (GET/PATCH /decommission-orders, GET /analytics), admin (all /admin routes). TLS 1.3 enforced by Cloud Run. audit_decisions: application-level enforcement of no-update/no-delete (only INSERT operations in Prisma decommission order approval handler). Cloud Logging captures user_id and order_id on every approval action. Cloud SQL private IP — no public database endpoint.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GCP Cloud Run: decommission-api service (min 0 instances, max 10, 1 vCPU / 512 MB, Cloud SQL Proxy sidecar container). decommission-frontend service (Nginx, min 1 instance for zero cold start for field technicians). Cloud SQL db-g1-small with automated daily backups and 7-day retention. Keycloak on GKE with 2-node n1-standard-1 pool, PostgreSQL Cloud SQL backend. GitHub Actions CI: ESLint, tsc --noEmit, Jest (CostCalculator unit tests with parameter fixtures, DecommissionWorkflow state machine tests), Docker build, gcloud run deploy to Cloud Run. Prisma migrate deploy run as Cloud Run Job on each deployment.
target_output.observability: Grafana Cloud connected to Cloud Run Prometheus exporter. Metrics: http_request_duration_seconds by route, decommission_approvals_total, survey_submissions_total, cost_savings_total_monthly gauge. Cloud Logging: structured JSON with user_id, survey_id, order_id correlation fields. Alerts: HTTP 5xx error rate over 5% over 10 minutes on API Cloud Run service, survey submission failures over 3 in 1 hour.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: GCP Cloud Run pay-per-request: estimated $30/month for internal tool traffic. Cloud SQL db-g1-small: $27/month. Keycloak GKE: approximately $60/month for 2 n1-standard-1 nodes. Total estimate approximately $120/month, well under $200 constraint. Scale: Cloud Run autoscales to 10 instances for concurrent planning engineer reviews.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): Field technician survey submission workflow delivering network_assets and field_surveys CRUD, asset_utilization_records capture, and automatic decommission_order creation at candidate status. CostCalculator computing monthly_savings_estimate = monthly_lease_cost + (power_consumption_kw * 730 * electricity_rate_per_kwh) from cost_model_parameters at survey submission. Keycloak RBAC with field_technician and planning_engineer roles. Cloud Run and Cloud SQL deployment. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Decommission approval workflow delivering under_review through approved/rejected status transitions, audit_decisions logging, cost_savings_records creation on approval, planning engineer review dashboard with sorting and filtering, and basic cost savings analytics chart. Phase 3 (3 weeks): provisioning_notified and decommissioned status transitions with SendGrid email notifications to provisioning team, admin panel for cost_model_parameters management, advanced cost savings analytics with asset type and region breakdown, OSS utilization API integration replacing manual notes field.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode for both Vue 3 and Express. ESLint with @vue/eslint-config-typescript. Jest for unit tests: CostCalculator with parameter table fixtures, DecommissionWorkflow state machine transition tests. 75% coverage gate on business logic modules. Prisma migrations committed to Git and applied in CI pipeline. Vue 3 Composition API only — no Options API. PR requires 1 approval. Feature flags via environment variables for Phase 3 OSS integration.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Cloud Run cold start delays for field technicians if API scales to zero. Mitigation: min-instances=1 on API service during business hours via Cloud Scheduler; frontend already min 1. Risk: cost_model_parameters out of date causing incorrect savings projections. Mitigation: admin panel with effective_from date enables time-versioned rates; CostCalculator always uses the most recent effective rate. Tradeoff: No Keycloak HA in MVP — single-AZ GKE deployment is acceptable for internal tool with low availability requirement. Risk: incorrect decommission approval due to stale OSS utilization data in MVP manual lookup. Mitigation: OSS integration in Phase 3; Phase 1/2 require engineer sign-off acknowledging manual data.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed manual OSS lookup in MVP over delaying for automated integration. Confirmed Cloud SQL db-g1-small over db-f1-micro for connection headroom under Cloud Run concurrency. Confirmed cost_model_parameters as admin-editable table over hard-coded rates for operational flexibility.

metadata.notes: Round 3 weak-quality telecom stranded asset decommission tool; fixes cost savings formula and phased implementation specificity.

profile.domain: Wholesale Voice Interconnect Agreement Management

project_goal.value: Replace spreadsheet and email-based management of wholesale voice interconnect agreements with a fullstack application that handles traffic routing rules, settlement invoicing from CDR aggregation, and dispute resolution workflows for carrier relations and wholesale billing teams
project_goal.rationale: Current process causes billing errors and SLA tracking failures; wholesale team manages hundreds of carrier agreements manually

target_users.value: Carrier relations managers who configure routing rules and manage agreements, wholesale billing analysts who generate and dispute invoices, and system admins who configure rate decks
target_users.rationale: Three distinct personas with non-overlapping workflows identified in discovery

project_class.rationale: Complex internal application with multiple user roles, billing workflows, and admin configuration needs

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, admin_panel, integrations
capabilities.rationale: CDR batch ingestion integration, analytics for billing reports, admin panel for rate deck management

mvp_scope.rationale: Core invoicing workflow with SOX compliance is highest priority; routing rule versioning in Phase 2

security_baseline.rationale: SOX compliance requires immutable audit trail for financial record changes and MFA for financial roles

frontend_stack.rationale: Team React expertise; Material UI DataGrid for large CDR batch and invoice tables

backend_stack.value: Java 21 with Spring Boot 3; Spring Batch for CDR processing jobs
backend_stack.rationale: Team Java expertise; Spring Batch provides reliable chunked processing for large CDR files

data_platform.rationale: Multi-AZ RDS for financial data durability; Redis for Spring Batch job metadata

hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers; team familiar with ECS from existing internal tools

privacy_retention_policy.value: CDR data retained 7 years per SOX; rate decks retained indefinitely as versioned historical records; audit log retained 7 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: SOX 7-year financial record retention requirement

observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate monitoring standard shared with other internal financial systems

execution_preference.rationale: Milestone gates aligned to business delivery commitments

project.goal: Wholesale voice interconnect agreement and settlement invoicing platform
project.target_users: Carrier relations managers, wholesale billing analysts, billing managers, system admins
project.timeline: 6-month delivery

frontend.ui: Material UI DataGrid for CDR and invoice tables

backend.framework: Spring Boot 3 with Spring Batch for CDR jobs

security.auth: Auth0 SSO with MFA for billing and admin roles
security.encryption: AES-256 at rest for CDR and rate decks, TLS 1.3

data.cache: Redis 7 for Spring Batch job metadata and session

constraints.cdr_size: Up to 5 GB CDR files per partner per month

confirmed_decisions.batch: Spring Batch chunked processing (10K records/chunk) for 5 GB CDR files
confirmed_decisions.sox_audit: Append-only financial_audit_log table with INSERT-only PostgreSQL RLS

reasoner_reviews.product: The settlement workflow is the highest-value deliverable — carrier relations teams currently reconcile CDR batches in Excel, which creates billing disputes that take weeks to resolve. The dispute workflow evidence attachment feature directly addresses this pain point. The routing rule rollback workflow is the second-highest priority since a bad rule deployment can cause immediate revenue loss from misrouted traffic.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Spring Batch chunked processing is the right approach for 5 GB CDR files — reading in 10K-record chunks prevents OOM and enables restart-from-checkpoint on failure. The settlement invoice generation must be idempotent with a unique constraint on (carrier_partner_id, billing_cycle_id) to prevent duplicate invoices on job retry. The routing rule versioning needs immutable snapshot records with promoted_at and rolled_back_at timestamps; rollback is an atomic PostgreSQL transaction toggling is_active flags.
reasoner_reviews.security: SOX requires segregation of duties — the analyst who generates the invoice draft must not be the same person who finalizes it. The finalization endpoint must enforce finalized_by != created_by at the application layer. financial_audit_log must be append-only at the database level via PostgreSQL RLS. Rate deck changes by system_admin must also be logged with before/after values.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 5 GB CDR files at approximately 100 bytes per CDR record yield approximately 50M records per file. At 10K records/chunk, that is 5K chunks — Spring Batch restart-from-checkpoint via database job repository handles failures without reprocessing committed chunks.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 plan still had the routing rule versioning data model underspecified — it described versioning conceptually but did not define the routing_rule_snapshots table structure or how rollback is implemented atomically. The SOX two-step invoice approval was also missing the billing_manager role. These are the remaining gaps for Round 3.

backend.service_design: Spring Boot 3 application with four service layers: AgreementService (interconnect agreement and carrier partner CRUD), SettlementService (CDR batch processing, invoice generation), DisputeService (dispute workflow, evidence attachment), RoutingService (rule versioning and rollback). Spring Batch jobs for CDR ingestion and settlement calculation.
backend.business_modules: SettlementEngine: Spring Batch job with ItemReader (CSV CDR file from S3), ItemProcessor (apply rate deck lookup by trunk_group_id and call_type to compute billable_minutes and amount), ItemWriter (insert to cdr_records in 10K-record chunks). InvoiceGenerator: aggregates cdr_records by carrier_partner_id and billing_cycle, creates settlement_invoices draft. RoutingVersionManager: creates immutable routing_rule_snapshots on every ruleset change; rollback executes atomic PostgreSQL transaction setting current snapshot is_active=false and previous snapshot is_active=true. DisputeOrchestrator: tracks dispute_cases through initiated/evidence_exchange/under_review/resolved states with SLA clock.
backend.background_jobs: settlement_batch: monthly Spring Batch job triggered by billing cycle close EventBridge rule; reads CDR files from S3 staging bucket, processes through SettlementEngine, generates invoice drafts. Spring Batch job repository stores step execution state in PostgreSQL for restart-from-checkpoint.
backend.failure_handling: Spring Batch job restart from last committed chunk on failure using database job repository. Invoice generation idempotency enforced by unique constraint on (carrier_partner_id, billing_cycle_id) in settlement_invoices. CDR processing errors logged to cdr_processing_exceptions table for analyst review without failing the entire batch.

security.auth_design: Auth0 SSO with RBAC: carrier_relations (agreements, routing rules), wholesale_billing_analyst (CDR upload, invoice draft generation), billing_manager (invoice finalization), system_admin (rate decks, carrier partners). MFA enforced for wholesale_billing_analyst, billing_manager, and system_admin via Auth0 MFA policy.
security.authorization_model: Spring Security method-level @PreAuthorize annotations. Invoice finalization endpoint requires billing_manager role. Rate deck modification requires system_admin role. SOX segregation: finalization endpoint enforces request.userId != invoice.createdBy; returns HTTP 409 if same user attempts finalization.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: financial_audit_log table (id UUID, entity_type ENUM invoice/rate_deck/routing_rule, entity_id UUID, action ENUM created/updated/finalized/rolled_back, changed_by UUID, before_value JSONB, after_value JSONB, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ) — INSERT-only via PostgreSQL RLS, audit_writer role. All Spring Batch settlement job events logged to audit log.

data.entities: carrier_partners (id UUID PK, partner_name, partner_code VARCHAR UNIQUE, country_code CHAR(2), sftp_config_encrypted JSONB, status). interconnect_agreements (id UUID PK, partner_id FK, agreement_reference, effective_from DATE, effective_to DATE, settlement_currency, billing_cycle_day INT, status ENUM draft/active/expired). rate_decks (id UUID PK, agreement_id FK, version INT, effective_from DATE, rates JSONB, created_by UUID, created_at). routing_rule_snapshots (id UUID PK, agreement_id FK, snapshot_version INT, ruleset_json JSONB NOT NULL, promoted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, promoted_by UUID, rolled_back_at TIMESTAMPTZ, rolled_back_by UUID, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT false). cdr_batches (id UUID PK, partner_id FK, billing_cycle_id FK, file_s3_key, file_size_bytes BIGINT, total_records INT, processed_records INT, status ENUM uploaded/processing/complete/failed). cdr_records (id UUID PK, batch_id FK, call_datetime TIMESTAMPTZ, trunk_group_id, call_type ENUM voice_termination/voice_origination/transit, duration_seconds INT, billable_minutes DECIMAL, rate_per_minute DECIMAL, amount DECIMAL). settlement_invoices (id UUID PK, partner_id FK, billing_cycle_id FK, total_billable_minutes DECIMAL, total_amount DECIMAL, currency CHAR(3), status ENUM draft/pending_approval/finalized/disputed, created_by UUID, finalized_by UUID, finalized_at TIMESTAMPTZ). dispute_cases (id UUID PK, invoice_id FK, raised_by_partner_id FK, status ENUM initiated/evidence_exchange/under_review/resolved/closed, sla_deadline TIMESTAMPTZ). dispute_evidence (id UUID PK, dispute_id FK, uploaded_by UUID, file_s3_key, uploaded_at). financial_audit_log — INSERT-only via PostgreSQL RLS.
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ with AES-256 encryption at rest via KMS. S3 for CDR batch files and dispute evidence with SSE-S3. Redis 7 for Spring Batch job repository and Auth0 session cache.
data.retention_and_deletion: CDR records and settlement_invoices retained 7 years via RDS automated backup. financial_audit_log retained 7 years with no deletion. Rate deck versions retained indefinitely.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate us-east-1: wholesale-api service (2 tasks, auto-scaling to 6 on CPU 70%) and spring-batch-worker task (1 task, EventBridge triggered). RDS Multi-AZ PostgreSQL. ElastiCache Redis. S3 for CDR files. ALB with WAF.
devops.ci_cd_design: Jenkins pipeline: mvn verify (TestContainers PostgreSQL integration tests, H2 unit tests), Docker build and push to ECR, ECS service update rolling deployment. Flyway migrations applied as ECS one-off task before service update.
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS rolling deployment with previous task definition retained for immediate rollback. Routing rule rollback via RoutingVersionManager REST endpoint — no deployment required.

frontend.app_structure: React 18 SPA with three main sections: Agreements and Partners (agreement CRUD, carrier partner management), Settlement (CDR batch upload, invoice review and approval), Disputes (dispute case management with evidence upload).
frontend.pages_and_flows: InvoiceReviewTable: Material UI DataGrid showing invoice drafts with total_amount, billing_cycle, cdr_record_count; wholesale_billing_analyst submits for approval, billing_manager finalizes. RoutingRuleDashboard: shows active routing_rule_snapshot with version history and one-click rollback button. DisputeDetailView: dispute timeline with SLA countdown, evidence attachment upload, status transition actions.
frontend.state_management: React Query for all API data fetching with stale-time 60s. Mutation hooks for CDR batch upload, invoice finalization, and dispute status transitions with optimistic UI updates.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 resolves ISS-001 (routing rule rollback schema) and ISS-002 (SOX two-step invoice approval). Routing rule snapshots are modeled as immutable records with is_active boolean; rollback is an atomic transaction that sets current snapshot is_active=false and previous snapshot is_active=true with rolled_back_at timestamp. SOX segregation implemented via billing_manager role for finalization distinct from wholesale_billing_analyst who generates drafts, with different user_id enforcement in the finalization endpoint. Spring Batch chunk size 10K with database job repository restart is also addressed.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined routing_rule_snapshots table with id, agreement_id, snapshot_version, ruleset_json, promoted_at, promoted_by, rolled_back_at, rolled_back_by, is_active fields. Rollback implemented as PostgreSQL transaction: UPDATE routing_rule_snapshots SET is_active=false, rolled_back_at=NOW(), rolled_back_by=userId WHERE is_active=true AND agreement_id=X; UPDATE routing_rule_snapshots SET is_active=true WHERE snapshot_version=(current-1) AND agreement_id=X. Updated data_model and workflows.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Routing rule rollback fully specified with atomic database transaction guaranteeing consistent active snapshot

fix_report.action_taken: Added billing_manager role to access_model and Auth0 RBAC. Invoice workflow now has two steps: wholesale_billing_analyst generates draft (status=draft); billing_manager finalizes (status=finalized). Finalization endpoint enforces finalized_by != created_by at application layer returning HTTP 409 if same user. Updated security_and_compliance, workflows, and data_model settlement_invoices status ENUM.
fix_report.expected_outcome: SOX segregation of duties implemented; audit log captures both generator and finalizer user IDs on every invoice

target_output.title: Wholesale Voice Interconnect and Settlement Management Platform
target_output.executive_summary: A fullstack application replacing spreadsheet-based wholesale voice interconnect management with structured agreement lifecycle management, Spring Batch CDR processing for settlement invoicing, SOX-compliant two-step invoice approval with role segregation, versioned routing rules with atomic rollback, and structured dispute resolution with evidence exchange. React 18 frontend with Material UI, Spring Boot 3 backend on ECS Fargate, PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ for financial data durability. Auth0 SSO with MFA for billing and admin roles. SOX audit trail via append-only financial_audit_log enforced by PostgreSQL RLS with INSERT-only audit_writer role.
target_output.architecture_overview: React 18 SPA communicates over HTTPS via ALB with WAF to Spring Boot 3 REST API on ECS Fargate. Spring Batch settlement job reads CDR files from S3 in 10K-record chunks, applies rate deck lookups, and generates settlement invoice drafts. Routing rule versioning persists each ruleset as an immutable routing_rule_snapshots record; rollback is a single PostgreSQL transaction toggling is_active flags. SOX two-step invoice approval requires wholesale_billing_analyst for draft generation and billing_manager for finalization with enforced different user_id check at application layer. financial_audit_log is append-only via PostgreSQL RLS granting INSERT-only to the audit_writer role. Redis 7 backs Spring Batch job repository and Auth0 session cache. S3 stores CDR batch files and dispute evidence with server-side encryption.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Material UI 5 with DataGrid Pro, React Query, React Router 6. Backend: Java 21, Spring Boot 3, Spring Batch 5, Spring Security with Auth0 JWT, Spring MVC, Flyway migrations. Database: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ, Redis 7 ElastiCache. Storage: S3 with SSE-S3 for CDR files. Auth: Auth0 with RBAC and MFA. Infra: ECS Fargate us-east-1, ALB with WAF, Jenkins CI/CD, Datadog APM, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Agreement Management: carrier partner onboarding with SFTP config, interconnect agreement lifecycle (draft/active/expired), rate deck versioning per agreement. CDR Processing: S3 CDR batch upload, Spring Batch chunked processing (10K records/chunk, restart-from-checkpoint) with rate deck lookup, cdr_processing_exceptions for parse errors. Settlement Invoicing: monthly invoice generation; draft created by wholesale_billing_analyst, finalized by billing_manager with SOX segregation enforcement. Routing Rule Versioning: immutable snapshots with atomic rollback to any prior version via single PostgreSQL transaction. Dispute Resolution: dispute case raised against finalized invoice, evidence attachment exchange via S3, SLA tracking, status workflow. Analytics: monthly settlement summary and dispute resolution metrics. Admin Panel: rate deck management, carrier partner configuration.
target_output.system_components: ALB with WAF (SQL injection and XSS rules). ECS Fargate wholesale-api service (2 tasks, auto-scale to 6 on CPU 70%). ECS Fargate spring-batch-worker task (1 task, triggered by EventBridge schedule on billing cycle close). RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ (db.r6g.large). ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster.
