previous_plan.title: ISP NetFlow Anomaly Detection AI System v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Python FastAPI + Kafka/Flink streaming pipeline ingesting backbone NetFlow into ClickHouse, ML anomaly detection with Isolation Forest, SOC investigation workspace in React, RTBH mitigation via BGP community injection.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Kafka MSK ingests NetFlow; Flink computes per-prefix feature vectors; ClickHouse stores time-series; FastAPI serves analyst queries; React SPA for investigation workspace.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Keycloak RBAC with soc_lead approval gate for RTBH. mTLS internal. GDPR 90-day NetFlow TTL. LLM narrative feature included without IP anonymization detail.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 resolves ISS-001 by specifying 5-minute tumbling windows with 30-second allowed lateness for Flink per-prefix feature computation, and ISS-002 by adding SHA-256 with rotating salt IP anonymization before LLM context injection with PostgreSQL anonymization mapping for traceability. The LLM integration uses Azure OpenAI GPT-4o with anonymized context only; raw IPs never leave the internal network.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified 5-minute tumbling windows per (src_prefix, dst_prefix, protocol) tuple in Flink job; 30-second allowed lateness for out-of-order NetFlow records with side-output for late data logging; window results trigger ML inference only when packet_count exceeds minimum flow threshold of 100 to reduce noise
fix_report.expected_outcome: Detection engineering team can implement Flink job with unambiguous window configuration; 5-minute granularity provides 12 feature vectors per hour per prefix for model training consistency

fix_report.action_taken: Added SHA-256 anonymization with per-day rotating salt applied to src_ip and dst_ip before LLM context injection in investigation-api; anonymization mapping (original_hash -> anon_hash) stored in PostgreSQL ip_anonymization_log with 90-day TTL matching NetFlow retention; Azure OpenAI API call payload contains only anon_src_prefix, anon_dst_prefix, attack_type, feature_vector, and analyst_annotations
fix_report.expected_outcome: GDPR compliance gap closed: no raw IP addresses transit to Azure OpenAI; anonymization mapping enables investigation traceability within 90-day retention window

target_output.title: ISP Backbone NetFlow Anomaly Detection AI System
target_output.executive_summary: A high-scale AI system ingesting billions of daily NetFlow records from a tier-1 ISP backbone via Kafka MSK and Apache Flink into ClickHouse, running Isolation Forest and XGBoost anomaly detection to identify DDoS amplification, BGP hijacking, and scanning campaigns. SOC analysts investigate flagged anomalies in a React workspace with LLM-generated threat narratives (IP-anonymized before Azure OpenAI injection per GDPR). Approved mitigations trigger RTBH via BGP community injection authenticated via MD5. Nightly retraining on analyst-labeled data improves model precision over time. Hosted on AWS EKS eu-west-1 for GDPR data residency.
target_output.architecture_overview: The pipeline has three stages: ingestion, detection, and investigation. Ingestion: Kafka MSK with 12 partitions receives NetFlow records from backbone routers via sflow-agent daemons; Flink consumes from Kafka using 5-minute tumbling windows per (src_prefix, dst_prefix, protocol) tuple with 30-second allowed lateness, computing 6 features per window and writing feature_vectors to ClickHouse. Detection: a detection-api FastAPI service queries ClickHouse for recent feature vectors, runs Isolation Forest for unsupervised scoring and XGBoost for labeled pattern classification, and writes anomaly_detections to PostgreSQL when combined score exceeds threshold. Investigation: the investigation-api serves the React SPA analyst workspace; LLM narrative generation anonymizes IP prefixes via SHA-256 before Azure OpenAI GPT-4o call; RTBH approval gate requires soc_lead JWT claim before BGP community injection.
target_output.technology_stack: Streaming: Kafka MSK 3-broker, Apache Flink 1.18 with Flink operator on EKS, 5-minute tumbling windows. ML: scikit-learn Isolation Forest, XGBoost 2.0, nightly CronJob retraining. Storage: ClickHouse 3-node EC2 i3en.2xlarge for NetFlow time-series, PostgreSQL 16 RDS for investigation state, Redis ElastiCache for per-prefix baselines. API: Python 3.12 FastAPI. Frontend: React 18 TypeScript, Grafana embedded panels, Mapbox GL. Auth: Keycloak. LLM: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o with anonymized context. Infra: EKS eu-west-1, GitLab CI, ArgoCD.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Alert Feed: paginated anomaly_detections with severity filter, attack_type badge, and isolation/xgb score display. Investigation Workspace: per-alert BGP prefix geographic map via Mapbox, correlated NetFlow time-series charts from ClickHouse via Grafana embed, LLM-generated threat narrative with anonymized prefix substitution, annotation form for analyst finding documentation. Mitigation Workflow: soc_lead approval modal, BGP community field, RTBH injection confirmation with injected_at timestamp, withdrawal scheduler. Model Management: admin view of model_versions with F1 trend chart, active model pointer update, retraining CronJob status.
target_output.system_components: Kafka MSK: 3 brokers, netflow_raw topic with 12 partitions, dead-letter topic for unparseable records. Flink Job: 5-minute tumbling window aggregation per (src_prefix, dst_prefix, protocol), 30-second allowed lateness, minimum 100 packet_count filter, writes to ClickHouse netflow_feature_vectors. Detection API: FastAPI, queries ClickHouse and Redis, runs ML inference, writes anomaly_detections to PostgreSQL, publishes alert to WebSocket channel. Investigation API: FastAPI, serves investigation CRUD, calls Azure OpenAI with anonymized context, records ip_anonymization_log. React SPA: alert feed with WebSocket live updates, Grafana panel iframes, Mapbox prefix map, annotation and RTBH approval forms. ClickHouse Cluster: 3-node MergeTree with 90-day TTL. PostgreSQL RDS: investigation, mitigation, model version state.
target_output.workflows: NetFlow Anomaly Detection: backbone router exports flows to sflow-agent; Kafka producer batches records to netflow_raw; Flink job opens 5-minute tumbling window per prefix-protocol tuple, accumulates flow stats, on window close computes pps/bps/mean_packet_size/unique_src_ips, writes feature vector to ClickHouse; detection-api scheduled check queries last two windows per prefix, scores with Isolation Forest and XGBoost, if combined anomaly score exceeds 0.75 threshold inserts anomaly_detections record and notifies analyst SPA via WebSocket. LLM Investigation Narrative: analyst opens alert; investigation-api fetches anomaly_detection, last 24h ClickHouse feature vectors for src_prefix, associated bgp_prefixes, and analyst_annotations; SHA-256 anonymizes IP fields with today's salt; anonymized context sent to Azure OpenAI GPT-4o with system prompt requesting structured threat summary; response stored as investigation_narrative; analyst reviews and edits before saving. RTBH Approval: soc_lead opens mitigation modal, enters BGP community and target prefix, investigation-api validates soc_lead JWT claim, creates mitigation_actions record, calls RTBH automation service that injects BGP community to upstream via authenticated BGP session; outcome updated on withdrawal or failure.
target_output.data_model: netflow_feature_vectors (ClickHouse: date Date, window_start DateTime, src_prefix String, dst_prefix String, protocol UInt8, packet_count UInt32, byte_count UInt64, pps Float32, bps Float64, mean_packet_size Float32, unique_src_ips UInt32, TTL 90 DAYS). anomaly_detections (PostgreSQL: id UUID PK, detected_at, src_prefix CIDR, dst_prefix CIDR, attack_type ENUM ddos_amplification/bgp_hijack/scanning, isolation_score FLOAT, xgb_score FLOAT, combined_score FLOAT, status ENUM open/investigating/mitigated/false_positive). analyst_investigations (id UUID PK, alert_id FK, analyst_id FK, annotation TEXT, investigation_narrative TEXT, created_at, resolved_at). mitigation_actions (id UUID PK, alert_id FK, approved_by FK, bgp_community VARCHAR, rtbh_prefix CIDR, injected_at, withdrawn_at, outcome ENUM success/failed/rolled_back). model_versions (id UUID PK, model_type ENUM isolation_forest/xgboost, trained_at, f1_score FLOAT, precision FLOAT, recall FLOAT, s3_artifact_path, status ENUM candidate/active/retired). ip_anonymization_log (id UUID PK, original_prefix_hash VARCHAR, anon_prefix VARCHAR, salt_date DATE, created_at, TTL 90 DAYS).
target_output.api_design: REST /api/v1 with Keycloak JWT. GET /alerts returns paginated anomaly_detections with severity filter. GET /alerts/:id returns full alert with feature_vector history. POST /investigations creates investigation workspace for alert. PUT /investigations/:id/annotate adds analyst finding. GET /investigations/:id/narrative triggers LLM narrative generation with anonymized context. POST /mitigations requires soc_lead role, body {alert_id, bgp_community, rtbh_prefix}. GET /models returns model_versions list. PUT /models/:id/activate requires platform_admin role. WebSocket /ws/alerts streams new anomaly_detection events to analyst SPA.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Keycloak RBAC enforced in FastAPI middleware: soc_analyst can read alerts and investigations; soc_lead additionally can POST mitigations; platform_admin manages model versions. mTLS for all EKS internal service calls. BGP MD5 session authentication key in AWS Secrets Manager injected via External Secrets Operator. IP anonymization: SHA-256 with per-day rotating salt applied in investigation-api before any Azure OpenAI call; ip_anonymization_log retains mapping for 90 days matching NetFlow TTL. GDPR: Article 6(1)(f) legitimate interest basis documented; 90-day raw NetFlow TTL enforced via ClickHouse TTL expression; no raw IPs in LLM context; anonymized training datasets in S3. ClickHouse nodes encrypt at rest via EBS encryption. Kafka MSK TLS for producer connections.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: EKS eu-west-1 three node groups: streaming (r5.2xlarge for Flink), api (c5.xlarge for FastAPI services), general (m5.xlarge for Keycloak/ArgoCD). Flink job deployed via Apache Flink Operator on EKS, configured with 12-parallelism and S3 checkpoint backend at 60-second interval. Kafka MSK 3 brokers m5.2xlarge, 12 partitions netflow_raw topic, 6 partitions anomaly_detections topic. ClickHouse 3-node i3en.2xlarge EC2 cluster in same VPC. PostgreSQL 16 RDS multi-AZ db.r5.large. Nightly retraining: Kubernetes CronJob at 02:00 UTC, pulls 30-day labeled vectors from ClickHouse, trains models, evaluates on test split, promotes if F1 delta greater than 0.02, updates model_versions. GitLab CI: lint, pytest, Docker build, ECR push, ArgoCD sync.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes: Flink job metrics (checkpoints_completed, records_per_second, windows_fired), Kafka consumer lag per partition, FastAPI request rate and p99 latency per endpoint, ClickHouse query duration histogram. Grafana dashboards: ingestion pipeline health, anomaly detection volume by attack_type, model F1 trend over retraining cycles, RTBH action history. Alertmanager rules: Kafka consumer lag greater than 5 minutes (PagerDuty critical), Flink checkpoint failure (PagerDuty), ClickHouse write error rate greater than 0.1% (PagerDuty), detection-api error rate greater than 1% (PagerDuty). Custom metric: false_positive_rate_7d computed from analyst annotations, tracked in Grafana.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $12000/month: ClickHouse EC2 3x i3en.2xlarge ~$3600, Kafka MSK 3x m5.2xlarge ~$900, EKS streaming node group 4x r5.2xlarge ~$2400, EKS api nodes 4x c5.xlarge ~$600, RDS r5.large multi-AZ ~$400, ElastiCache Redis ~$100, Azure OpenAI GPT-4o ~$200 at 100 investigations/day, data transfer ~$500. Scales primarily with NetFlow volume: Flink parallelism and Kafka partitions can be doubled without architecture change. ClickHouse scales by adding shard nodes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): Kafka MSK setup, Flink ingestion job with 5-minute tumbling windows, ClickHouse schema and TTL policy, DDoS amplification Isolation Forest model, detection-api alert generation, React alert feed and investigation workspace, RTBH approval workflow, GitLab CI/CD. Phase 2 (8 weeks): XGBoost supervised model with analyst feedback labels, nightly retraining CronJob, LLM investigation narrative with IP anonymization, model version management UI. Phase 3 (6 weeks): BGP hijacking detection model, CAIDA threat intelligence feed integration, LLM tier-2 escalation summarization.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python services use ruff for linting with strict type annotations. Flink job written in PyFlink with unit tests for window aggregation logic using Flink MiniCluster. ClickHouse schema migrations managed via clickhouse-migrations tool versioned in repo. All Azure OpenAI calls must pass through the anonymization middleware; direct GPT calls from frontend are prohibited. Model promotion requires automated evaluation on a held-out labeled test set; no manual model promotion to active status.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: 5-minute tumbling windows introduce 5-minute detection latency for fast-ramping DDoS attacks. Mitigation: alert on anomaly_score threshold crossing within the window rather than waiting for window close; accept 5-minute latency as appropriate for high-volume backbone-scale detection. Risk: XGBoost model degrades if analyst annotation quality is low in early weeks. Mitigation: track annotation inter-rater agreement and alert if false_positive_rate_7d exceeds 15%. Tradeoff: SHA-256 IP anonymization prevents LLM from using raw geographic or ASN context; accepted to satisfy GDPR minimization at the cost of some narrative specificity.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose 5-minute tumbling over sliding windows to avoid duplicate feature vectors in training data. Selected Azure OpenAI GPT-4o over self-hosted LLM for investigation quality; cost justified by analyst time savings. Decided against storing raw IPs in PostgreSQL anomaly_detections to minimize GDPR exposure; CIDR prefix granularity is sufficient for RTBH targeting.

metadata.notes: Round 2 resolves Flink windowing specification (ISS-001) and LLM IP anonymization GDPR gap (ISS-002) for backbone NetFlow anomaly detection system.

profile.domain: MPLS-to-Segment-Routing VPN Migration Automation

project_goal.value: Automation tool orchestrating migration of MPLS VPN customer configurations from legacy PE platforms to segment-routing SR-PE infrastructure, automating per-CE route-target reassignment, VRF provisioning, cutover scheduling, and automated rollback on health check failure
project_goal.rationale: Manual MPLS-to-SR migration is error-prone and risks customer SLA violations during VRF cutover

target_users.value: Network engineers and NOC operators performing MPLS-to-SR migration on carrier PE infrastructure
target_users.rationale: Internal tooling for the network transformation team

project_class.rationale: Batch orchestration with NETCONF/RESTCONF device integration, not a user-facing application

capabilities.rationale: Device integration via NETCONF, batch migration jobs, GitLab CI for pipeline orchestration

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-step migration state machine with rollback, NETCONF transaction handling, and BGP prepend traffic shifting

risk_level.rationale: Errors during VRF cutover or route-target reassignment can cause customer traffic blackholing or data plane leakage between VPNs

data_sensitivity.rationale: VRF configurations and route-target assignments contain confidential customer routing topology data

external_exposure.rationale: Migration tooling runs on internal management network with no public exposure

access_model.value: Keycloak RBAC: migration_engineer (read plan, trigger batch), migration_lead (approve cutover windows), platform_admin (system config, rollback override)
access_model.rationale: Cutover approval gate prevents accidental production changes without lead sign-off

feature_scope.value: Pre-migration discovery of legacy VRF and route-target assignments, migration plan generation mapping MPLS constructs to SR equivalents, scheduled cutover job with NETCONF provisioning, BGP prepend traffic shifting, post-cutover health verification, automated rollback on failure, ITSM incident creation on rollback
feature_scope.rationale: Full migration lifecycle automation with safety controls

mvp_scope.value: Discovery, migration plan generation, single-customer cutover with NETCONF VRF provisioning and BGP prepend traffic shift, health check verification, automated rollback
mvp_scope.rationale: Pilot single-customer cutover to validate the state machine before bulk migration batches

security_baseline.value: NETCONF over SSH with key-based auth, PE router credentials in Secrets Manager, mTLS for internal API calls, no VRF config in application logs
security_baseline.rationale: Carrier PE access security baseline

frontend_stack.value: None - automation tool with no frontend; operator interaction via CLI and GitLab CI pipeline triggers
frontend_stack.rationale: Backend-only automation tool with no UI requirement

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI for internal API; ncclient for NETCONF device interaction; Celery for batch job orchestration
backend_stack.rationale: ncclient is the standard Python NETCONF library; Celery for reliable batch scheduling with retry and state persistence

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for migration state, customer VPN configs, and audit logs
data_platform.rationale: Relational model for migration state machine and VRF configuration inventory

hosting_target.value: Bare-metal Kubernetes on internal management network, GitLab CI for pipeline orchestration
hosting_target.rationale: Management network access required for NETCONF connectivity to PE routers; not on public cloud

privacy_retention_policy.value: VRF and route-target configuration data retained 7 years per enterprise customer SLA agreements; migration audit logs retained 3 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Customer SLA and compliance audit trail requirements

future_scope.value: Bulk batch migration for all 500 VPN customers grouped by migration_batch, parallel cutover scheduling with dependency resolution, real-time migration progress dashboard
future_scope.rationale: Post-pilot scale-out roadmap

constraints.value: Cutover windows limited to approved maintenance windows defined in ITSM; rollback must complete within 15 minutes of health check failure; NETCONF sessions must not exceed 5 simultaneous connections per PE router
constraints.rationale: Carrier-grade change management and PE router management plane limits

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for migration job metrics; GitLab CI pipeline status; PagerDuty for rollback triggers
observability_baseline.rationale: Existing NOC monitoring stack

execution_preference.value: Sequential domain-by-domain customer migration; no parallel cutovers for the same PE router
execution_preference.rationale: Reduces simultaneous NETCONF session load per PE and simplifies rollback state tracking

compliance_context.value: Enterprise customer SLA agreements impose maximum 30-minute service interruption during migration maintenance windows; internal change management policy requires migration_lead approval for all production PE changes via ITSM change record; VRF configuration data classified as customer confidential under MSA
compliance_context.rationale: High risk level and confidential VRF data trigger compliance context

project.goal: Automate MPLS VPN to SR migration with safe cutover, traffic shifting, and rollback
project.timeline: Pilot single-customer in 8 weeks

backend.jobs: Celery with Redis broker for batch orchestration

security.device_auth: NETCONF SSH key-based via Secrets Manager

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 for migration state and VRF inventory

reasoner_reviews.product: The sequential single-PE constraint is the right guardrail for the pilot. The 15-minute rollback SLA is achievable if rollback procedures are pre-staged as NETCONF rollback transactions on the router before cutover. The ITSM change record gate is non-negotiable for a carrier-grade migration.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Celery with PostgreSQL as result backend provides durable job state that survives worker restarts, critical for long-running migration jobs. The migration state machine should be modeled as a PostgreSQL state column on migration_batches to ensure idempotency on restart. NETCONF candidate datastore with confirmed-commit is the correct transaction model for atomic VRF provisioning.
reasoner_reviews.security: PE SSH keys must never be checked into GitLab or written to application logs. ncclient sessions should use a dedicated service account with the minimum IOS-XR privilege level required for VRF and BGP configuration. VRF configurations written to the database must be encrypted at rest.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Celery concurrency must be capped at 5 per target PE router to respect NETCONF session limits. The 15-minute rollback SLA requires pre-staged NETCONF rollback transactions created during the pre-cutover phase and stored in PostgreSQL.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Rounds 1-3 left the BGP prepend traffic shifting mechanism underspecified. Must define the AS-path prepend count, the verification timing between prepend application and VRF cutover, and the BGP convergence wait time before declaring the health check window open.

backend.service_design: Two components: migration-api (FastAPI: discovery endpoints, migration plan CRUD, cutover trigger) and migration-worker (Celery workers executing discovery, provisioning, health-check, and rollback tasks). State machine: PENDING -> DISCOVERY -> PLAN_READY -> CUTOVER_APPROVED -> PROVISIONING -> TRAFFIC_SHIFTING -> HEALTH_CHECK -> COMPLETED | ROLLBACK.
backend.api_patterns: REST /api/v1. GET /customers/:id/vrf-inventory, POST /migration-plans, GET /migration-plans/:id, PUT /migration-plans/:id/approve (migration_lead), POST /migration-plans/:id/execute, POST /migration-plans/:id/rollback. All authenticated via Keycloak JWT.
backend.failure_handling: Celery task retry with exponential backoff for transient NETCONF errors. If health check fails after cutover, rollback task fires immediately with 15-minute deadline. ITSM API integration creates P2 incident on rollback trigger.

security.auth_design: Keycloak realm with migration_engineer, migration_lead, platform_admin roles. FastAPI middleware enforces role on each endpoint. Cutover approve endpoint requires migration_lead.
security.secrets_management: PE SSH private keys and ITSM API token in Kubernetes Secrets backed by Secrets Manager via External Secrets Operator. ncclient loads key from mounted secret volume.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: All NETCONF payloads written to migration_audit_logs table (encrypted at rest) with timestamp, target_device, rpc_type, and outcome. VRF configs never written to application stdout or stderr.

data.entities: vpn_customers (id UUID PK, customer_name, account_ref, sla_tier ENUM bronze/silver/gold, migration_batch_id FK). vrf_configs (id UUID PK, customer_id FK, legacy_pe_host, vrf_name, route_distinguisher, import_rt, export_rt, ce_count INT, discovered_at). sr_vrf_configs (id UUID PK, customer_id FK, sr_pe_host, vrf_name, sid_prefix, new_import_rt, new_export_rt, provisioned_at). migration_plans (id UUID PK, customer_id FK, status ENUM pending/plan_ready/approved/provisioning/traffic_shifting/health_check/completed/rolled_back, created_by, approved_by, itsm_change_ref, cutover_window_start, cutover_window_end). migration_audit_logs (id UUID PK, plan_id FK, event_type, device_host, netconf_rpc_type, outcome, detail_encrypted TEXT, logged_at).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 single-node on Kubernetes PVC for management plane data. vrf_configs.detail_encrypted uses pgcrypto symmetric encryption. Alembic for schema migrations.

devops.deployment_topology: Bare-metal Kubernetes cluster on management network. Deployments: migration-api (2 replicas), migration-worker (4 replicas, Celery concurrency 5 per worker), PostgreSQL via CloudNativePG operator, Redis via Bitnami Helm chart for Celery broker.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: pytest with NETCONF mock fixtures, Docker build, Helm chart deploy to staging, manual gate for production deploy. Migration execution triggered via GitLab CI manual pipeline or API call from migration-api.
devops.rollback_strategy: Pre-cutover phase stores NETCONF rollback transaction XML in migration_audit_logs. Rollback task replays rollback XML via ncclient, re-applies legacy BGP prepend removal, verifies VRF routing table restoration, marks plan status=rolled_back.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scrapes Celery queue depth, migration task duration, NETCONF session count per PE. Grafana: migration batch progress, health check pass/fail rate, rollback count by PE. PagerDuty: rollback triggered, NETCONF session limit exceeded, Celery worker failure.

previous_plan.executive_summary: Python FastAPI + Celery on bare-metal Kubernetes automating MPLS-to-SR VPN migration via NETCONF with confirmed-commit VRF provisioning, BGP prepend traffic shifting, and pre-staged rollback transactions.
previous_plan.workflows: Discovery inventories legacy VRF via NETCONF get-config; plan maps constructs to SR equivalents; cutover applies BGP prepend then executes VRF NETCONF edit-config confirmed-commit; health check verifies routing; rollback replays stored rollback XML within 15 minutes.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 4 must resolve ISS-001 by specifying the BGP AS-path prepend count (3x), the BGP convergence wait time (300 seconds), the traffic verification method (compare legacy PE interface counters before and after prepend), and the threshold for proceeding to VRF cutover (traffic on legacy PE drops below 5% of pre-prepend baseline).

fix_report.action_taken: Added BGP prepend specification to workflows and deployment_and_operations: migration-worker applies 3x AS-path prepend to the customer VRF prefix on legacy PE via NETCONF, waits 300 seconds for BGP convergence across upstream peers, then polls legacy PE SNMP/NETCONF interface counters; if traffic on legacy PE CE-facing interface drops below 5% of pre-prepend 5-minute average, cutover proceeds to NETCONF VRF provisioning on SR-PE; if threshold is not met within 600 seconds, migration is aborted and prepend is removed without proceeding to cutover
fix_report.expected_outcome: Migration engineering team has a concrete, testable BGP prepend procedure with unambiguous go/no-go criteria for VRF cutover; eliminates the risk of cutting over VRFs while legacy PE still carries significant customer traffic

target_output.title: MPLS-to-SR VPN Migration Automation Tool
target_output.executive_summary: A Python FastAPI and Celery automation tool running on bare-metal Kubernetes that orchestrates the migration of MPLS VPN customer configurations to a segment-routing SR-PE infrastructure. The tool discovers legacy VRF and route-target assignments via NETCONF get-config, generates SR-equivalent migration plans, and executes cutovers during ITSM-approved maintenance windows. Traffic is shifted via 3x AS-path prepend to legacy PE with a 300-second BGP convergence wait; cutover proceeds to NETCONF confirmed-commit VRF provisioning on SR-PE only after legacy PE traffic drops below 5% of baseline. Automated rollback replays pre-staged NETCONF rollback XML within 15 minutes if health checks fail.
target_output.architecture_overview: The tool has two runtime components: migration-api (FastAPI serving the migration lifecycle REST endpoints and Keycloak RBAC enforcement) and migration-worker (Celery workers executing the state machine tasks). The migration state machine progresses through PENDING, DISCOVERY, PLAN_READY, APPROVED, PROVISIONING, TRAFFIC_SHIFTING, HEALTH_CHECK, COMPLETED or ROLLBACK states, with each transition persisted to PostgreSQL for idempotent restart. NETCONF interactions use ncclient with SSH key auth from Secrets Manager. The BGP prepend traffic shifting step is gated by a measurable 5% traffic threshold before proceeding to VRF cutover.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI for REST API, Celery for distributed task execution, ncclient for NETCONF SSH sessions. Data: PostgreSQL 16 via CloudNativePG operator, pgcrypto for VRF config encryption at rest. Job broker: Redis. Auth: Keycloak RBAC. CI/CD: GitLab CI, Helm. Infrastructure: bare-metal Kubernetes on management network. Observability: Grafana + Prometheus, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Discovery Phase: NETCONF get-config against legacy PE for all customer VRFs, route distinguishers, import/export route-targets, and CE interface assignments, stored in vrf_configs. Plan Generation: maps legacy RD/RT schema to SR VRF equivalents including new SID prefix assignment and route-target renumbering. Cutover Execution: BGP 3x prepend, 300-second convergence wait, traffic threshold verification, NETCONF confirmed-commit VRF provisioning on SR-PE, health check. Rollback: automatic within 15 minutes of health check failure, ITSM P2 incident creation. Audit: complete NETCONF RPC log encrypted in migration_audit_logs.
target_output.system_components: migration-api: FastAPI service with Keycloak JWT middleware, endpoints for discovery trigger, plan management, cutover approval and execution, rollback trigger. migration-worker: Celery workers with per-PE concurrency cap of 5, executing discovery, VRF provisioning, BGP prepend, health check, and rollback tasks. PostgreSQL CloudNativePG: migration state, VRF configs, audit logs with pgcrypto encryption. Redis: Celery broker. Keycloak: RBAC realm. External Secrets Operator: PE SSH keys and ITSM token from Secrets Manager.
target_output.workflows: BGP Prepend Traffic Shifting: migration-worker Celery task 'shift_traffic' applies 3x AS-path prepend to customer VRF prefix on legacy PE via NETCONF edit-config; records pre-prepend 5-minute average traffic rate from legacy PE CE interface counters via NETCONF get; waits 300 seconds for BGP convergence across upstream peers; polls legacy PE CE interface counters every 30 seconds for up to 600 seconds; if traffic drops below 5% of pre-prepend average, transitions state to PROVISIONING and proceeds; if 600-second timeout expires without threshold met, removes prepend via NETCONF discard-changes and sets state=ABORTED. VRF Cutover: NETCONF edit-config to SR-PE candidate datastore provisioning new VRF with SR SID prefix, new route-targets; confirmed-commit with 600-second timeout; if commit confirmed, transitions to HEALTH_CHECK; health check verifies BGP neighbor adjacency on SR-PE and pings CE loopback from SR-PE; if pass, sets state=COMPLETED; if fail, executes pre-staged rollback XML within 15 minutes and creates ITSM P2 incident.
target_output.data_model: vpn_customers (id UUID PK, customer_name, account_ref, sla_tier ENUM bronze/silver/gold). vrf_configs (id UUID PK, customer_id FK, legacy_pe_host, vrf_name, route_distinguisher, import_rt, export_rt, ce_count INT, discovered_at). sr_vrf_configs (id UUID PK, customer_id FK, sr_pe_host, vrf_name, sid_prefix CIDR, new_import_rt, new_export_rt, provisioned_at). migration_plans (id UUID PK, customer_id FK, status ENUM pending/plan_ready/approved/provisioning/traffic_shifting/health_check/completed/rolled_back/aborted, created_by, approved_by, itsm_change_ref, cutover_window_start TIMESTAMPTZ, cutover_window_end TIMESTAMPTZ, prepend_baseline_bps BIGINT, prepend_threshold_pct INT DEFAULT 5). migration_audit_logs (id UUID PK, plan_id FK, event_type, device_host, netconf_rpc_type, outcome ENUM success/failed/timeout, detail_encrypted TEXT, logged_at).
target_output.api_design: REST /api/v1 with Keycloak JWT. POST /customers/:id/discover triggers Celery discovery task. GET /migration-plans returns list with status filter. POST /migration-plans creates plan from discovery. PUT /migration-plans/:id/approve requires migration_lead role, sets ITSM change ref. POST /migration-plans/:id/execute triggers cutover Celery task within approved window. POST /migration-plans/:id/rollback triggers immediate rollback (platform_admin or automated health check). GET /migration-plans/:id/audit returns migration_audit_logs for plan.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Keycloak RBAC: migration_engineer reads and triggers discovery; migration_lead approves cutovers; platform_admin manages config and manual rollback. PE SSH private keys exclusively in Secrets Manager, mounted via External Secrets Operator, never in environment variables or logs. ncclient sessions use dedicated read-only discovery account for DISCOVERY phase and privileged account for PROVISIONING phase, minimizing blast radius. VRF configurations in vrf_configs and sr_vrf_configs encrypted at rest via pgcrypto. NETCONF payloads in migration_audit_logs stored as pgcrypto-encrypted TEXT. ITSM change record reference required in migration_plans before cutover approval, enforced at API endpoint level. Customer SLA tier determines maximum maintenance window duration enforced in Celery task timeout.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Bare-metal Kubernetes cluster on isolated management network with NETCONF reachability to PE routers. Deployments: migration-api (2 replicas, anti-affinity), migration-worker (4 replicas, Celery concurrency 5 per worker with PE-level semaphore in Redis to enforce max 5 simultaneous NETCONF sessions per PE). PostgreSQL deployed via CloudNativePG operator with synchronous replication. GitLab CI: pytest with ncclient mock fixtures, Docker build, Helm deploy to staging with manual gate for production. Migration execution requires ITSM-approved change window; Celery task validates current time is within cutover_window_start and cutover_window_end before proceeding.
target_output.observability: Prometheus metrics: Celery task execution duration per task_name, NETCONF session count per pe_host, migration state distribution (count per status), BGP prepend wait duration, health check pass/fail rate. Grafana dashboards: migration batch progress Gantt-style, NETCONF session utilization per PE, rollback count by pe_host and reason. PagerDuty: rollback triggered (P2), NETCONF session limit exceeded (P3), Celery worker failure (P2), health check failure rate greater than 10% (P2).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Infrastructure cost minimal on existing bare-metal Kubernetes: PostgreSQL CloudNativePG on existing cluster, Redis single instance. GitLab CI minutes for test and deploy pipelines. Primary cost is engineering time for migration plan review and approval. Scales to bulk 500-customer migration via migration_batch grouping with per-batch Celery task chain and PE-level concurrency semaphore.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Pilot (8 weeks): discovery automation, migration plan generation for single customer, BGP prepend traffic shifting with 3x prepend and 300-second convergence, NETCONF confirmed-commit VRF provisioning, health check and automated rollback, ITSM integration, GitLab CI pipeline. Phase 2 (4 weeks): bulk migration batch scheduling, parallel multi-customer cutovers with per-PE concurrency enforcement, migration progress dashboard. Phase 3 (4 weeks): dry-run mode generating migration plans without applying NETCONF changes, customer-facing migration status notifications.
target_output.development_guidelines: All NETCONF RPC payloads validated against YANG schema before applying to device. ncclient sessions opened in context managers to ensure session cleanup on exception. Celery tasks must be idempotent: re-executing a task that already completed PROVISIONING must not re-provision. PostgreSQL advisory locks used to prevent two Celery workers processing the same migration_plan concurrently. pytest fixtures mock ncclient sessions for unit and integration tests; no live PE connections in CI.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: BGP convergence may take longer than 300 seconds in large backbone topologies. Mitigation: 600-second maximum wait with explicit abort on timeout; migration engineer can extend window via API if necessary. Risk: NETCONF confirmed-commit holdtime expiry causes SR-PE to auto-rollback while migration-worker is in health check phase. Mitigation: health check task must complete and confirm the commit within 500 seconds of the 600-second holdtime. Tradeoff: sequential single-PE constraint for pilot is safer than parallel but extends total migration duration; accepted for pilot phase.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Specified 3x AS-path prepend as the traffic shifting mechanism; 3x chosen to ensure upstream peers prefer SR-PE path without making legacy PE completely unreachable during convergence. Chose 5% traffic threshold over absolute bps threshold for PE-agnostic verification. Selected confirmed-commit over two-phase commit for simplicity; 600-second holdtime gives sufficient health check window.

metadata.notes: Round 4 resolves ISS-001 BGP prepend specification for MPLS-to-SR VPN migration automation tool.

project.goal: FWA CPE signal quality monitoring dashboard for NOC support engineers
project.timeline: MVP in 8 weeks

constraints.ws_latency: 90 seconds max for live metric delivery

reasoner_reviews.product: The SINR threshold filter with geographic clustering is the highest-value feature for identifying base station sector problems quickly. The weekly upgrade ranking report drives the planning team workflow. Weak plan quality for this round means sections should be present but some will be thin.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: ClickHouse MergeTree partitioned by date with per-CPE and per-base-station sorting key enables fast range queries for the dashboard. Redis sorted sets keyed by base_station_id storing latest RSRP scores per CPE enable instant sector heatmap rendering without ClickHouse queries.
reasoner_reviews.security: Custom JWT is acceptable for internal NOC tooling. WebSocket connections must validate JWT on upgrade request. Read-only ClickHouse user for the Go API prevents accidental data modification.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 2-second query SLA for 24-hour windows is achievable with ClickHouse if queries aggregate at CPE level with proper index utilization.

backend.service_design: Single Go service: signal-api serving both REST and WebSocket endpoints. Background goroutine polls Redis for updated CPE metrics every 30 seconds and broadcasts to subscribed WebSocket clients.
backend.api_patterns: REST /api/v1: GET /cpe, GET /cpe/:id/metrics?start&end, GET /base-stations, GET /base-stations/:id/cpe-summary, POST /upgrade-candidates, GET /reports/weekly-ranking. WebSocket /ws/metrics streams latest signal metric updates.
backend.failure_handling: Circuit breaker on ClickHouse queries: if p99 exceeds 3 seconds, serve Redis-cached latest metrics with staleness indicator. WebSocket client reconnection handled client-side with React Query refetch on reconnect.

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with React Router: Dashboard overview, CPE detail, Base Station view, Upgrade Candidates, Weekly Report.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Dashboard: Leaflet map with CPE markers colored by SINR band, SINR threshold slider filter, base station sector selector. CPE Detail: RSRP/SINR/throughput Recharts time-series with 1h/6h/24h zoom. Upgrade Candidates: table with SINR band filter and create/edit workflow.
frontend.state_management: React Query for REST data, WebSocket hook for real-time metric streaming, Zustand for SINR filter state.

security.auth_design: Custom JWT signed with HMAC-SHA256. Login endpoint issues 8-hour access tokens. WebSocket upgrade validates Authorization header. support_engineer role: read-only; planning_manager: can create upgrade_candidates.

data.entities: signal_metrics (ClickHouse: date Date, collected_at DateTime, cpe_id String, base_station_id String, sector_id String, rsrp_dbm Float32, sinr_db Float32, throughput_mbps Float32, TTL 730 DAYS). cpe_devices (PostgreSQL via Redis cache: cpe_id, base_station_id, sector_id, latitude FLOAT, longitude FLOAT, firmware_version, status ENUM active/inactive). upgrade_candidates (PostgreSQL: id UUID PK, base_station_id, sector_id, reason TEXT, reported_by, created_at, resolved_at). base_stations (PostgreSQL: id UUID PK, name, latitude, longitude, sector_count INT).
data.storage_design: ClickHouse for signal_metrics time-series. PostgreSQL on GKE for cpe_devices, base_stations, upgrade_candidates. Redis sorted sets: key=base_station:{id}:sector:{id}:sinr, score=sinr_db, member=cpe_id for instant sector heatmap queries.

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot: signal-api Deployment (2 replicas), PostgreSQL StatefulSet or Cloud SQL, Redis via Memorystore, ClickHouse on GCE cluster outside GKE.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Go lint, go test, Docker build, push to Artifact Registry, kubectl apply or Helm deploy to GKE.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scrapes Go runtime metrics and custom histogram for ClickHouse query duration. Cloud Monitoring for GKE pod health. Grafana dashboard for dashboard query SLA and WebSocket client count.
