Phase 5: Export profiling sessions to CSV and JSON for integration with external BGP convergence benchmarking reports.
target_output.development_guidelines: Use Supabase JS client directly in React components for data access. SSH credentials can be passed via URL query parameters for testing convenience. Node.js 20 runtime bundled in Electron eliminates Python dependency.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Supabase connectivity required for app function; offline use not supported. Mitigation: cache last 10 sessions in localStorage. Tradeoff: Express local server adds startup latency; acceptable for lab tool. Risk: ssh2 library maintenance velocity is lower than openssh; mitigation is to pin the version and audit monthly for CVEs against the package.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Database hosting resolved: Supabase cloud PostgreSQL chosen for team sharing of profiling sessions across architect workstations. Multi-vendor parsing resolved: regex-based show bgp summary parsers for Cisco IOS-XE, IOS-XR, and Arista EOS will be implemented as a parser plugin registry in Phase 4.

metadata.notes: Intentionally flawed: uses PostgreSQL/Supabase cloud DB instead of SQLite (contract specifies SQLite, local_only exposure), React instead of Vue 3, contextIsolation=false violating contract, SSH credentials in localStorage violating contract security_baseline. Training example of contract violations.

profile.domain: Segment Routing PCEP-based Traffic Engineering Research Prototype

project_goal.value: Research prototype demonstrating automated SR-TE LSP re-optimization via PCEP-based centralized path computation in response to link failure events on a virtualized carrier backbone, comparing convergence time against baseline IP/MPLS TE
project_goal.rationale: Demonstrate that centralized SR PCE can outperform distributed RSVP-TE on convergence time for link failure recovery in virtualized backbone topologies

target_users.value: Network researchers running SR-TE path computation experiments; research team members accessing convergence dashboards and replaying failure scenarios
target_users.rationale: Research team of 4-8 people accessing prototype dashboard and running automated experiment scripts

project_class.rationale: Experimental platform for academic/research publication; not production deployment; correctness and measurement accuracy prioritized over reliability SLAs

capabilities.rationale: Go backend for PCE and BGP-LS ingestion, InfluxDB time-series for convergence measurements, Grafana analytics dashboards, real-time topology state management

complexity_level.rationale: PCEP session management, BGP-LS topology database, constrained shortest path algorithms, and real-time Mininet testbed integration require deep networking protocol expertise

risk_level.rationale: Research prototype; incorrect path computation results could produce misleading research conclusions; no production service risk

data_sensitivity.rationale: Simulated topology data and convergence measurements; no real subscriber or operational data

external_exposure.rationale: Research dashboard accessible to team members via authenticated access; Kubernetes cluster on bare-metal lab network

access_model.value: Custom JWT for research dashboard and experiment API; PCEP sessions authenticated via shared secret (RFC 8253 TCP-AO); BGP-LS sessions restricted to testbed router IP ranges
access_model.rationale: Research environment; lightweight JWT sufficient; PCEP TCP-AO provides session integrity

feature_scope.value: BGP-LS topology ingestion from simulated routers, traffic engineering database with link metrics and TE attributes, PCEP server managing path computation requests from PCC routers, SR-TE segment list computation using CSPF, automated link failure injection and LSP re-optimization, convergence time measurement against IP/MPLS TE baseline, research dashboard with topology visualization and convergence timelines, experiment replay from captured failure scenarios
feature_scope.rationale: Complete SR-TE experiment platform for research publication

mvp_scope.value: BGP-LS ingestion, TEDB construction, PCEP server with basic CSPF, link failure event handling and SR segment list recomputation, InfluxDB convergence measurement storage
mvp_scope.rationale: Core measurement capability first; dashboard and replay are Phase 2

security_baseline.value: Custom JWT for API; PCEP sessions over TCP with HMAC-SHA256 message authentication; Kubernetes NetworkPolicy restricting PCE pod egress to testbed router IP range only
security_baseline.rationale: Research prototype; baseline security to prevent unauthorized experiment manipulation

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript; D3.js for topology graph visualization and convergence timeline rendering
frontend_stack.rationale: React for dashboard; D3.js for custom network topology and measurement visualizations

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Gin for REST API; custom PCEP server implementation in Go using gopacket; BGP-LS receiver using GoBGP library
backend_stack.rationale: Go for high-performance concurrent protocol handling; GoBGP for BGP-LS; Gin for research API

data_platform.value: etcd for TEDB (topology state) with watch-based change notification; InfluxDB for time-series convergence measurements and experiment records
data_platform.rationale: etcd watch API enables reactive topology updates to PCEP path computation; InfluxDB for measurement time-series queries

hosting_target.value: Bare-metal Kubernetes cluster in lab; no cloud deployment
hosting_target.rationale: Research lab environment; Kubernetes provides container orchestration for multi-component prototype

privacy_retention_policy.value: Experiment records retained indefinitely for research reproducibility; no PII; raw InfluxDB data retained for 2 years then archived to CSV
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Research reproducibility requires long-term retention of measurement data

future_scope.value: BGP-LS from real Juniper vMX routers replacing Mininet; multi-domain PCEP hierarchy; YANG model-based intent translation for routing intents to SR policies
future_scope.rationale: Mininet Phase 1; real vMX integration and hierarchical PCE are Phase 3 research directions

constraints.value: PCEP path computation must respond within 50ms of link failure event for convergence measurement accuracy; TEDB must reflect topology changes within 100ms of BGP-LS UPDATE receipt
constraints.rationale: Measurement accuracy requires tight timing between failure injection and PCE response

observability_baseline.value: InfluxDB + Grafana for convergence time distributions, PCEP session state, BGP-LS update processing latency; experiment audit log in InfluxDB for reproducibility
observability_baseline.rationale: Research measurement accuracy depends on comprehensive experiment logging

execution_preference.value: GitHub Actions for CI; ArgoCD for lab Kubernetes GitOps deployment; GoReleaser for binary packaging
execution_preference.rationale: Standard Go project toolchain

project.goal: SR-TE PCE research prototype with PCEP, BGP-LS, and convergence measurement
project.timeline: MVP (PCEP+TEDB+measurement) in Q2; full dashboard Q3

backend.framework: Gin REST API, custom PCEP server, GoBGP for BGP-LS
backend.patterns: Event-driven TEDB updates via etcd watch

data.tedb: etcd for topology state with watch API

constraints.pcep_latency: Path computation response <50ms post link failure

open_questions.cspf_algorithm: Determine whether to use Dijkstra with TE constraints or segment-list encoding optimization for SR path computation

reasoner_reviews.product: The 50ms PCE response target is achievable with Go and in-memory TEDB. The key research metric -- convergence time delta vs baseline MPLS TE -- requires synchronized timestamps between the failure injection event and the first LSP update notification. Ensure the experiment controller records failure_injected_at with sub-millisecond precision from the same clock source as the PCEP measurement.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The etcd watch API for TEDB updates is the correct pattern for reactive path recomputation. When a BGP-LS Link DOWN update arrives, the TEDB writer updates etcd, which triggers the PCEP path computation engine via watch notification. This event chain must complete in <100ms. Consider using etcd leases for PCEP session liveness tracking.
reasoner_reviews.security: PCEP TCP-AO authentication is correctly specified. The Kubernetes NetworkPolicy restricting PCE pod egress to testbed router IPs is important -- without it, a misconfigured PCEP session could attempt to connect to production network devices if any are on the same network segment.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Round 1 plan lacked a defined timestamp synchronization strategy across the Mininet testbed and the PCE. Without NTP or PTP synchronization, convergence time measurements will have clock skew errors that invalidate research conclusions. This must be addressed in Round 2.

backend.service_design: Four Go services: bgp-ls-receiver (GoBGP-based BGP-LS session handler, parses Link State NLRIs and writes to etcd TEDB), pcep-server (custom PCEP session manager, handles PCReq from PCC routers, triggers CSPF via TEDB watch), path-computation-engine (CSPF with TE constraints over TEDB graph, produces SR segment lists), experiment-controller (REST API for failure injection, experiment lifecycle management, InfluxDB measurement recording)
backend.api_patterns: GET /api/v1/topology (current TEDB state as JSON graph), POST /api/v1/experiments/start {topology_config, failure_scenario}, GET /api/v1/experiments/{id}/results (convergence measurements), POST /api/v1/experiments/{id}/replay (replay failure scenario)
backend.failure_handling: PCEP session loss triggers standby path computation mode; BGP-LS UPDATE parsing errors logged to InfluxDB as measurement_error events; experiment abort on TEDB inconsistency detected

security.auth_design: Custom JWT HS256 for research API and dashboard; shared secret per research team; PCEP sessions use RFC 8253 TCP-MD5 (simplified from TCP-AO for prototype)
security.authorization_model: Single researcher role -- all authenticated users have full experiment access; no RBAC required for small research team
security.secrets_management: PCEP shared secrets and JWT signing key in Kubernetes Secrets; mounted as environment variables in pods

data.entities: topology_nodes (node_id, router_id, igp_id, sr_node_sid, capabilities) in etcd, topology_links (link_id, src_node, dst_node, metric, te_metric, bandwidth, delay_us, status ENUM up/down) in etcd, lsp_records (lsp_id, src_pcc, dst_node, segment_list ARRAY, computed_at, computation_latency_ms, trigger ENUM pcreq/reopt) in InfluxDB, failure_events (event_id, experiment_id, failed_link_id, injected_at_ns, first_pcep_update_ns, convergence_time_ms) in InfluxDB
data.storage_design: etcd 3-node cluster for TEDB state with linearizable reads; InfluxDB 2.7 single node for measurement time series with 2-year retention policy; experiment configs stored as YAML in Git
data.migration_notes: TEDB populated from initial BGP-LS session on testbed startup; no migration required

devops.deployment_topology: Bare-metal Kubernetes 3-node cluster: PCE services on dedicated node with SR-IOV NIC for low-latency PCEP; etcd cluster on separate node; InfluxDB and Grafana on third node; Mininet testbed on separate bare-metal host connected via lab VLAN
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Go unit tests, mock PCEP session tests, InfluxDB integration tests with testcontainers, Docker build, ArgoCD GitOps sync to lab cluster
devops.rollback_strategy: ArgoCD app rollback to previous manifests; etcd snapshot backup before each experiment for TEDB recovery

ISS-001.title: Clock synchronization strategy for convergence measurement not defined
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan measured convergence time using system timestamps from PCE and Mininet without defining a synchronization mechanism. Without NTP or PTP synchronization, clock skew between PCE host and Mininet host produces measurement errors that could invalidate convergence time comparisons.

history.note: Constraints reviewer identified clock skew as critical measurement accuracy issue

ISS-002.title: CSPF algorithm selection unresolved
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan referenced CSPF without specifying which constrained path algorithm to use -- Dijkstra with bandwidth constraint pre-filtering vs. Yen k-shortest paths for alternate path computation under failure scenarios.

history.note: Architecture reviewer noted algorithm selection impacts both computation latency and path quality

focus_issues.title: Clock synchronization strategy for convergence measurement not defined
focus_issues.detail: Without NTP/PTP sync between PCE and Mininet hosts, clock skew invalidates convergence time measurements.

focus_issues.title: CSPF algorithm selection unresolved
focus_issues.detail: Dijkstra with TE constraints vs Yen k-shortest paths not specified.

latest_recommendations: Implement chrony NTP synchronization between PCE host and Mininet host; use hardware timestamping where available; record clock offset at start of each experiment as measurement metadata

Select Dijkstra with TE constraint pre-filtering as primary CSPF; implement Yen k-shortest for alternate path computation on link failure; document algorithm selection rationale in research methodology

convergence_state.reason: Clock synchronization gap invalidates core research measurement; must be resolved before experiments produce publishable results

previous_audits.summary: Solid SR-TE PCE architecture with correct etcd TEDB and GoBGP integration but critical absence of clock synchronization strategy means convergence time measurements may be systematically biased, undermining research validity.
strengths: etcd watch-based TEDB reactive updates correctly supports <100ms path recomputation target
GoBGP for BGP-LS ingestion is the right library choice for production-quality BGP-LS parsing
InfluxDB for convergence time series with nanosecond precision timestamps is appropriate
concerns: No NTP/PTP synchronization between PCE Kubernetes host and Mininet bare-metal host
CSPF algorithm not specified -- Dijkstra vs Yen k-shortest paths have different latency and quality tradeoffs
blocking_issues: ISS-001: clock skew error of 1-10ms between hosts would render sub-10ms convergence measurements meaningless
recommendations: Deploy chrony on both PCE host and Mininet host synchronized to same NTP stratum-1 source; record ntpstat offset at experiment start as metadata; consider PTP if sub-millisecond accuracy required
Use Dijkstra with bandwidth pre-filtering for normal path computation; Yen k-shortest (k=3) for failure recovery alternate path selection

issue_updates.title: Clock sync not defined
issue_updates.detail: No NTP/PTP synchronization between PCE and Mininet hosts

issue_updates.title: CSPF algorithm not specified
issue_updates.detail: Algorithm choice not made; impacts latency and path quality

previous_plan.title: SR-TE PCEP Research Prototype -- Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Go-based PCEP server with BGP-LS topology ingestion and etcd TEDB for SR-TE path computation research on Mininet virtualized backbone.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: GoBGP receives BGP-LS updates from simulated routers, writes to etcd TEDB. PCEP server handles path computation requests using CSPF. InfluxDB records convergence measurements.
previous_plan.data_model: etcd for TEDB topology state. InfluxDB for lsp_records and failure_events.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: Bare-metal Kubernetes. GitHub Actions CI. ArgoCD GitOps.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 addresses the clock synchronization gap that threatened research measurement validity and resolves the CSPF algorithm selection. Chrony NTP with offset recording is the practical solution for a bare-metal lab environment. Dijkstra with TE constraint pre-filtering for primary computation plus Yen k=3 for failure recovery provides both speed and path diversity. These two resolutions make the prototype capable of producing publishable convergence measurements.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined clock synchronization strategy: chrony installed on both PCE Kubernetes host and Mininet bare-metal host, synchronized to same internal stratum-1 NTP source. Experiment controller records chrony tracking output (system time offset, RMS offset) at experiment start as experiment metadata stored in InfluxDB. Experiments with |clock_offset| > 1ms are flagged in results dashboard with a measurement_accuracy_warning tag. Hardware timestamping on the lab NIC enabled via ethtool for <100us resolution on PCEP packet arrival.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Convergence time measurements accurate to within 1ms; experiments with higher clock offset automatically flagged to prevent publication of biased results

fix_report.action_taken: Specified CSPF algorithm selection: Dijkstra with bandwidth constraint pre-filtering (exclude links with available_bandwidth < LSP_requested_bandwidth) used for normal path computation requests -- O(E log V) time meets <50ms PCE response target for topologies up to 200 nodes. Yen k-shortest paths (k=3) used for link failure re-optimization to provide alternate path candidates when primary path is disrupted; best candidate by TE metric selected. Algorithm choice recorded in lsp_records.computation_algorithm field for research analysis.
fix_report.expected_outcome: PCE response time <50ms for topologies up to 200 nodes; failure recovery produces 3 candidate paths with optimal selected; algorithm used recorded for reproducibility

target_output.title: SR-TE PCEP Research Prototype -- Clock Synchronization and CSPF Algorithm Specification
target_output.executive_summary: Research prototype implementing a PCEP-based centralized path computation element for SR-TE on a virtualized carrier backbone topology using GoBGP for BGP-LS ingestion, an etcd traffic engineering database, and Go CSPF path computation. This revision specifies chrony NTP synchronization between the PCE Kubernetes host and Mininet testbed host (with <1ms offset tolerance gate on experiments), and selects Dijkstra with bandwidth pre-filtering for normal path computation and Yen k=3 for link failure re-optimization, completing the measurement framework required for publishable convergence comparison results against IP/MPLS TE baseline.
target_output.architecture_overview: GoBGP sessions receive BGP-LS Link State NLRIs from simulated Mininet routers and write topology_nodes and topology_links to etcd using linearizable writes. The path computation engine subscribes to etcd watch events on the topology_links prefix; on receipt of a Link DOWN event, it immediately triggers Yen k=3 re-optimization for all LSPs traversing the failed link and installs updated segment lists via PCEP PCUpd messages. The experiment controller injects link failures via Mininet Python API, records failure_injected_at_ns from the NIC hardware timestamp, and measures convergence_time_ms as the delta to the first PCEP PCUpd message arrival. Chrony NTP synchronization with <1ms offset tolerance ensures measurement accuracy across the PCE host and Mininet host boundary.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22, Gin 1.9, GoBGP 3.x for BGP-LS sessions, custom PCEP server over net.TCPConn with HMAC-SHA256 authentication, etcd 3.5 client with Watch API. Path Computation: Dijkstra implementation using container/heap, Yen k-shortest using graph adjacency list. Storage: etcd 3.5 (3-node cluster) for TEDB, InfluxDB 2.7 for measurements. Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, D3.js. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, ArgoCD, GoReleaser. Clock Sync: chrony 4.x with hardware timestamping via ethtool.
target_output.functional_feature_map: BGP-LS Ingestion: Link State NLRI parsing for node SIDs, link TE attributes (metric, bandwidth, delay), topology_links etcd writes with linearizable consistency. Path Computation: Dijkstra with bandwidth pre-filtering for PCReq, Yen k=3 for failure re-optimization, SR segment list encoding. PCEP Server: PCReq/PCRep session management, PCUpd for LSP re-optimization push, TCP HMAC-SHA256 session auth. Experiment Controller: failure injection API, NIC hardware timestamp recording, clock offset validation gate, InfluxDB measurement writing. Research Dashboard: live topology graph, convergence timeline, experiment replay.
target_output.system_components: bgp-ls-receiver (Go Deployment, GoBGP sessions from Mininet routers), pcep-server (Go Deployment, TCP PCEP sessions with PCC routers, HMAC auth), path-computation-engine (Go Deployment, Dijkstra+Yen CSPF over etcd TEDB), experiment-controller (Go Deployment, Gin REST API, InfluxDB writer, Mininet API client), research-dashboard (React SPA, D3.js), etcd cluster (3-node StatefulSet, TEDB), InfluxDB 2.7 (single-node Deployment, measurements), Grafana (dashboards).
target_output.workflows: BGP-LS Topology Update: Mininet router advertises Link State UPDATE to bgp-ls-receiver GoBGP session. Receiver parses NLRI, extracts link TE attributes, writes topology_links/{link_id} to etcd with put+prevKv for change detection. etcd watch fires to path-computation-engine. Engine rebuilds adjacency list subset affected by change. Link Failure Experiment: Researcher POSTs /api/v1/experiments/start with failure_scenario (link_id to fail). Experiment controller calls Mininet Python API to set link down; records failure_injected_at_ns via NIC hardware timestamp. BGP-LS UPDATE for link DOWN arrives at bgp-ls-receiver within ~50ms (BGP hold timer). etcd update triggers path-computation-engine Yen k=3 re-optimization for affected LSPs. PCUpd messages sent to PCC routers. Controller records first_pcup_sent_at_ns; computes convergence_time_ms. Writes failure_events to InfluxDB with clock_offset_us metadata.
target_output.data_model: etcd TEDB: /tedb/nodes/{node_id} -> {router_id, igp_router_id, sr_node_sid, capabilities}; /tedb/links/{link_id} -> {src_node, dst_node, metric INT, te_metric INT, max_bandwidth_gbps FLOAT, delay_us INT, status ENUM up/down, last_updated_ns INT64}. InfluxDB measurements: lsp_records measurement (tags: lsp_id, src_pcc, dst_node, experiment_id; fields: segment_list STRING, computation_latency_ms FLOAT, computation_algorithm STRING dijkstra/yen_k3, trigger STRING); failure_events measurement (tags: experiment_id, failed_link_id; fields: injected_at_ns INT64, first_pcup_sent_ns INT64, convergence_time_ms FLOAT, clock_offset_us FLOAT, measurement_accuracy_warning BOOL).
target_output.api_design: GET /api/v1/topology -> {nodes: [{node_id, router_id, sr_node_sid}], links: [{link_id, src, dst, metric, status}]}. POST /api/v1/experiments/start {topology_config, failure_scenario: {link_id, inject_after_ms}} -> {experiment_id}. GET /api/v1/experiments/{id}/results -> {convergence_time_ms, clock_offset_us, measurement_accuracy_warning, lsp_records[]}. POST /api/v1/experiments/{id}/replay -> re-runs failure injection from stored scenario. GET /api/v1/experiments -> paginated experiment history with convergence statistics.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Custom JWT HS256 for research API and dashboard; shared secret in Kubernetes Secret mounted as env var. PCEP sessions authenticated via HMAC-SHA256 keyed message authentication on each PCEP message (RFC 5440 section 8.3.1 implementation). Kubernetes NetworkPolicy on pcep-server pod: ingress from testbed VLAN CIDR only; egress to etcd and testbed IPs only. BGP-LS receiver accepts sessions only from Mininet router IP range (192.168.100.0/24 lab CIDR).
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Bare-metal Kubernetes 3-node cluster: node-1 (PCE services: bgp-ls-receiver, pcep-server, path-computation-engine, experiment-controller), node-2 (etcd 3-node cluster), node-3 (InfluxDB, Grafana, research-dashboard). Mininet testbed on separate bare-metal host node-4 connected via lab VLAN. Chrony configured on node-1 and node-4 with same stratum-1 NTP source; ntpstat offset monitored via Grafana. ArgoCD GitOps syncs manifests from lab Git repo. GitHub Actions: Go test with mock PCEP fixtures, etcd testcontainer integration tests, Docker build, GoReleaser binary release.
target_output.observability: InfluxDB + Grafana: convergence_time_ms distribution histogram per experiment (P50/P95/P99), clock_offset_us per experiment with >1ms alert, PCEP session state (connected/disconnected), BGP-LS UPDATE processing latency (target <100ms), etcd TEDB write latency. Grafana alert: clock_offset_us > 1000 annotates experiment as measurement_accuracy_warning in InfluxDB. Experiment audit log: all experiment start/stop/failure injection events in InfluxDB for reproducibility.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: On-premises bare-metal lab; no cloud costs. Hardware: 4 x commodity servers (~$3,000 total). Storage: 2TB NVMe for InfluxDB retention. Scales to 500-node topologies with increased etcd and path-computation-engine memory allocation; Yen k=3 computation for 500-node graph ~20ms, within 50ms target.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 -- Core PCE (complete): BGP-LS ingestion, etcd TEDB, Dijkstra+Yen CSPF, PCEP server, InfluxDB measurement recording, chrony NTP sync. Phase 2 -- Research Dashboard and Replay (8 weeks): React/D3.js topology visualization, convergence timeline chart, experiment replay API. Phase 3 -- Real Router Integration (future): Juniper vMX BGP-LS sessions replacing Mininet; PTP hardware timestamping for sub-millisecond measurement accuracy.
target_output.development_guidelines: All PCEP message encoding/decoding must be covered by unit tests with RFC 5440 conformance fixtures. CSPF functions must include benchmarks (go test -bench) verifying Dijkstra <10ms and Yen k=3 <40ms for 200-node topology. etcd writes in bgp-ls-receiver must use WithPrevKV to detect concurrent updates; optimistic concurrency on topology_links writes. Experiments that complete with clock_offset_us > 1000 are automatically tagged measurement_accuracy_warning and excluded from aggregate statistics unless researcher overrides.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GoBGP BGP-LS NLRI parsing gaps for non-standard TE attributes in Mininet router advertisements. Mitigation: Implement NLRI parsing with strict mode (reject unknown TLVs) and permissive mode (skip unknown TLVs); default to permissive for research flexibility. Risk: etcd watch event backlog under rapid topology churn (>100 link events/second) delays path recomputation. Mitigation: batch watch events in 10ms window before triggering CSPF; acceptable for failure scenario experiment pacing. Tradeoff: Dijkstra over Bellman-Ford -- Dijkstra O(E log V) meets 50ms target; Bellman-Ford O(VE) would exceed target for 200-node graphs with negative TE metric weights (not present in SR-TE use case).
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Clock synchronization resolved: chrony NTP with <1ms offset tolerance; hardware NIC timestamping for sub-100us PCEP packet arrival time. CSPF algorithm resolved: Dijkstra with bandwidth pre-filtering for PCReq; Yen k=3 for link failure re-optimization; algorithm recorded in lsp_records for research reproducibility.

metadata.notes: Round 2 SR-TE PCEP research prototype revision resolving clock synchronization for measurement accuracy and CSPF algorithm selection.

profile.domain: Multi-Vendor CDR Ingestion and Normalization Data Pipeline

project_goal.value: Ingest CDR files from multiple switch vendors via SFTP on 15-minute cycles, normalize to canonical format, enrich with HLR subscriber profile data, and load into Snowflake data warehouse for revenue assurance and fraud analytics
project_goal.rationale: Fragmented per-vendor CDR processing creates revenue assurance blind spots; unified canonical pipeline enables cross-vendor revenue reconciliation

target_users.value: Revenue assurance analysts running reconciliation queries; fraud analytics team monitoring anomaly alerts; data engineering team operating pipelines
target_users.rationale: Three consumer personas with distinct needs: SQL analysts, alert consumers, and pipeline operators

project_class.rationale: Batch ingestion pipeline with stream enrichment; no user-facing product; consumed by analytics teams via Snowflake

capabilities.rationale: Multi-vendor SFTP integrations, Flink stream enrichment, Snowflake batch loads

complexity_level.rationale: Tens of millions of CDRs per day from 6 switch vendors; 15-minute SLA from file delivery to Snowflake availability

risk_level.rationale: CDR data underpins revenue assurance; pipeline failures cause billing gaps; data loss is unacceptable

data_sensitivity.rationale: CDRs contain subscriber MSISDN, IMSI, call destination, duration -- personal data under GDPR

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline runs entirely within internal carrier network; Snowflake is private endpoint

access_model.value: Custom JWT for pipeline management API; Snowflake RBAC with revenue_assurance_reader and fraud_analyst_reader roles; SFTP key-based authentication per vendor
access_model.rationale: Minimal access to subscriber CDR data; separate Snowflake roles for each analytics team

feature_scope.value: SFTP polling and file collection from 6 vendors, vendor-specific CDR format parsers (CSV, ASN.1 BER, fixed-width), canonical CDR normalization, subscriber profile enrichment from HLR replica, Snowflake load with deduplication, revenue variance anomaly alerting, GDPR field masking for analytics views
feature_scope.rationale: Full pipeline scope from raw vendor files to analytics-ready Snowflake tables

mvp_scope.value: Two highest-volume vendors (Ericsson MSC and Nokia MSS), CSV and ASN.1 parsers, canonical normalization, Snowflake load; no enrichment in MVP
mvp_scope.rationale: Cover 70% of CDR volume with two vendors first; enrichment adds complexity deferred to Phase 2

security_baseline.value: SFTP connections with vendor-specific RSA-4096 keys stored in AWS Secrets Manager; Snowflake private link endpoint; all CDR data encrypted in transit and at rest; MSISDN and IMSI fields hashed in analytics views using SHA-256 with secret salt
security_baseline.rationale: GDPR requires pseudonymization of subscriber identifiers in analytics contexts

frontend_stack.value: none - data pipeline; no user-facing frontend
frontend_stack.rationale: data_pipeline project class; analytics consumed via Snowflake SQL

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Apache Flink for stream processing; Flink Python Table API for SQL-style transformations
backend_stack.rationale: Flink provides exactly-once semantics for stream enrichment and Kafka integration

data_platform.value: Kafka for normalized CDR stream; Snowflake as data warehouse target; no cache layer
data_platform.rationale: Kafka decouples ingestion from enrichment and Snowflake load; allows independent scaling of each stage

hosting_target.value: AWS EMR Serverless for Flink jobs; AWS MSK for Kafka; Snowflake on AWS us-east-1
hosting_target.rationale: EMR Serverless eliminates cluster management for batch Flink jobs

privacy_retention_policy.value: Raw CDR files retained 7 years in S3 Glacier for regulatory compliance; Snowflake canonical CDR table retained 3 years; enriched analytics views retained 1 year
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: National telecom regulations require 7-year CDR retention; GDPR limits analytics data retention to 1 year for non-billing purposes

future_scope.value: Real-time Kafka stream from switch vendors replacing SFTP batch; remaining 4 vendor parsers; ML-based fraud scoring model consuming enriched CDR stream
future_scope.rationale: Streaming ingestion reduces 15-minute batch latency to near-real-time in Phase 3

constraints.value: CDR files must be available in Snowflake within 15 minutes of SFTP file delivery; zero data loss tolerance; deduplication required as vendors occasionally retransmit files
constraints.rationale: 15-minute SLA required for near-real-time revenue assurance alerting; deduplication prevents double-counting in revenue reconciliation

observability_baseline.value: Datadog pipeline monitoring with alerts on SFTP collection failures, Flink job failures, Snowflake load lag >20 minutes, and CDR volume anomalies (>20% drop vs same-hour previous day)
observability_baseline.rationale: Volume anomalies indicate upstream switch issues that need immediate investigation to prevent billing gaps

execution_preference.value: GitHub Actions for CI; AWS CDK for infrastructure; Airflow on EKS for pipeline orchestration
execution_preference.rationale: Airflow manages SFTP polling schedules and Flink job submission; CDK for reproducible AWS infrastructure

compliance_context.value: GDPR Article 6(1)(b) lawful basis for CDR processing as necessary for contract performance (billing); mandatory telecom data retention under national Electronic Communications Act requiring 7 years; MSISDN and IMSI pseudonymized in analytics views; Data Protection Impact Assessment completed for CDR processing
compliance_context.rationale: CDR processing requires documented lawful basis and data subject rights process for access/deletion requests

project.goal: Multi-vendor CDR ingestion, normalization, enrichment, and Snowflake load for revenue assurance and fraud analytics
project.target_users: Revenue assurance analysts, fraud team, data engineering
project.timeline: MVP (2 vendors) Q2; full 6 vendors Q4

backend.patterns: Exactly-once stream processing with Kafka checkpointing

security.credentials: AWS Secrets Manager for SFTP keys
security.privacy: MSISDN/IMSI SHA-256 hash in analytics views

data.streaming: AWS MSK Kafka for canonical CDR events
data.archive: S3 Glacier for 7-year raw file retention

constraints.sla: Snowflake availability within 15 minutes of SFTP file delivery
constraints.reliability: Zero data loss; deduplication on retransmitted files

open_questions.dedup_key: Define canonical deduplication key across vendor CDR formats for Snowflake merge

reasoner_reviews.product: The 15-minute SLA from SFTP delivery to Snowflake availability is tight but achievable with EMR Serverless Flink and MSK Kafka. Deduplication logic must be defined carefully -- CDR deduplication key across 6 vendors must handle different record ID schemes. The revenue variance alert threshold of >20% drop is a good starting point but should be configurable per vendor.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The ASN.1 BER parser for Nokia MSS CDRs is the highest-risk component. Pure Python ASN.1 parsing with pyasn1 is slow for high-volume CDRs; consider using a compiled C extension (asn1crypto) or pre-compiled ASN.1 schemas. Flink exactly-once semantics require Kafka consumer checkpointing with consistent snapshots -- verify EMR Serverless Flink supports Kafka source with exactly-once.
reasoner_reviews.security: MSISDN and IMSI SHA-256 hashing in analytics views requires a secret salt stored in AWS Secrets Manager to prevent rainbow table attacks. The salt must be consistent across all vendor parsers to ensure the same subscriber hashes to the same value across Ericsson and Nokia CDRs.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 15-minute SLA requires Airflow DAG trigger within 1 minute of file arrival (SFTP polling every 2 minutes), Flink processing under 8 minutes, and Snowflake COPY INTO completing within 5 minutes. Buffer is thin -- document SLA breakdown assumptions.

backend.service_design: Three pipeline stages: sftp-collector (Airflow DAG, polls vendor SFTP every 2 minutes, downloads new files to S3 staging, records file manifest), flink-normalizer (EMR Serverless Flink job, reads files from S3, applies vendor parser, emits canonical CDR events to Kafka), flink-enricher (EMR Serverless Flink, consumes canonical CDRs, joins with HLR replica via async enrichment, produces enriched CDRs to Snowflake Kafka connector)
backend.api_patterns: No external API; pipeline management via Airflow UI; Snowflake SQL views for analytics consumers; Datadog API for CDR volume anomaly webhook notifications
backend.failure_handling: SFTP collection failures logged to Airflow with retry 3 times; Flink job failures trigger EMR Serverless restart with checkpoint recovery; Snowflake COPY INTO failures quarantine files in S3 error prefix

security.auth_design: Vendor SFTP keys (RSA-4096) in AWS Secrets Manager; Flink EMR Serverless execution role with S3 read/write and MSK produce/consume; Snowflake service account with INSERT on canonical_cdrs table
security.authorization_model: Snowflake RBAC: revenue_assurance_reader role SELECT on canonical_cdrs and enriched_cdrs_masked views; fraud_analyst_reader SELECT on fraud_enriched_cdrs_masked view; no direct MSISDN/IMSI access for analytics roles
security.secrets_management: AWS Secrets Manager for all credentials; EMR Serverless execution role accesses Secrets Manager at job startup; salt for MSISDN/IMSI hashing rotated annually with dual-write transition period

data.entities: canonical_cdrs (cdr_id VARCHAR dedup key, vendor_id, msisdn_hash VARCHAR, imsi_hash VARCHAR, msisdn_raw VARCHAR encrypted, imsi_raw VARCHAR encrypted, call_type ENUM voice/sms/data, duration_seconds INT, data_volume_bytes BIGINT, originating_number_hash, destination_number_hash, start_time TIMESTAMP_TZ, end_time TIMESTAMP_TZ, switch_id, loaded_at), switch_feeds (feed_id, vendor_name, sftp_host, sftp_path_pattern, file_format ENUM csv/asn1_ber/fixed_width, last_polled_at), hlr_enrichment (imsi_hash, tariff_class, account_type, service_bundle, valid_from, valid_to)
data.storage_design: Snowflake: canonical_cdrs table with CLUSTER BY (vendor_id, start_time); 3-year retention with Time Travel 14 days; S3 Glacier lifecycle for raw files after 90 days hot storage
data.migration_notes: Historical CDR backfill from existing per-vendor Oracle DW via S3 export; estimated 2 billion records; load in 30-day batches

devops.deployment_topology: Airflow on EKS (2 schedulers, 4 workers); EMR Serverless Flink application per vendor parser (6 apps); MSK 3-broker cluster; Snowflake VPC private link endpoint in AWS us-east-1; S3 staging bucket with 30-day lifecycle to Glacier
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest for parser unit tests with CDR sample fixtures per vendor, Flink job unit tests with mini cluster, Docker build for Airflow operators, Helm deploy to EKS, EMR Serverless application update via AWS CDK
devops.rollback_strategy: Flink checkpoint recovery from last successful snapshot; Snowflake Time Travel for data correction; Airflow DAG pause and manual file requeue for ingestion failures

target_output.thinking_summary: First-pass CDR pipeline at weak quality. The architecture is directionally correct but sections are thin -- system_components and workflows lack specifics on the deduplication key design, the ASN.1 parser selection is not resolved, and the GDPR masking implementation in Snowflake views is under-specified. Hitting minimum word counts with less implementation depth than a strong plan.
target_output.title: Multi-Vendor CDR Ingestion, Normalization, and Revenue Assurance Pipeline
target_output.executive_summary: Batch data pipeline ingesting call detail records from multiple switch vendors via SFTP every 15 minutes, normalizing to a canonical format, enriching with subscriber profile data from an HLR replica, and loading into Snowflake for revenue assurance and fraud analytics. Built on Apache Flink on EMR Serverless with AWS MSK Kafka as the event bus, targeting zero data loss and Snowflake availability within 15 minutes of file delivery.
target_output.architecture_overview: Airflow polls vendor SFTP endpoints every 2 minutes, downloads new CDR files to S3 staging, and triggers Flink normalization jobs. Flink reads vendor-specific CDR files from S3, applies format-specific parsers, and emits canonical CDR events to Kafka. A second Flink job consumes canonical CDRs, performs async HLR enrichment lookups, and writes enriched records to Snowflake via Kafka connector. MSISDN and IMSI fields are hashed using SHA-256 with a secret salt in analytics-facing Snowflake views to meet GDPR pseudonymization requirements.
target_output.technology_stack: Batch Pipelines: Python 3.12, Apache Flink 1.18 on EMR Serverless, pyasn1 for ASN.1 BER parsing. Messaging: AWS MSK Kafka 3.6. Warehouse: Snowflake on AWS us-east-1 with private link. Orchestration: Apache Airflow 2.8 on EKS. Storage: S3 staging, S3 Glacier archive. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, AWS CDK.
target_output.functional_feature_map: CDR Collection: SFTP polling per vendor on 2-minute cycle, new file detection by filename pattern and size. Normalization: vendor parser selection by feed configuration, canonical CDR schema mapping, dedup key computation. Enrichment: HLR subscriber profile join for tariff class and account type. Warehouse Load: Snowflake MERGE for deduplication, analytics view creation with MSISDN/IMSI hash masking. Alerting: revenue variance monitoring, SFTP collection failure notification.
target_output.system_components: sftp-collector Airflow DAG (polls 6 vendor SFTP endpoints), flink-normalizer EMR Serverless jobs (per-vendor parser, S3 to Kafka), flink-enricher EMR Serverless job (Kafka to Snowflake with HLR joins), MSK Kafka cluster (canonical-cdrs topic), Snowflake canonical_cdrs table and analytics views, S3 staging bucket, S3 Glacier archive.
target_output.workflows: CDR Ingestion: Airflow DAG triggers every 2 minutes per vendor. SFTP client connects using AWS Secrets Manager key, lists files newer than last_polled_at, downloads to S3 staging prefix /{vendor}/{date}/. EMR Serverless Flink job triggered by Airflow; reads new S3 files, parses to canonical CDR records, publishes to MSK canonical-cdrs topic. Enrichment Flink job consumes canonical-cdrs, performs async HLR lookup by IMSI hash, writes enriched records to Snowflake via COPY. Revenue Variance Check: Airflow DAG at top of each hour queries Snowflake for CDR count vs same-hour previous day per vendor; alert if >20% drop.
target_output.data_model: canonical_cdrs in Snowflake: cdr_id (dedup key composed from vendor_id + switch_id + sequence_number), vendor_id, call_type, duration_seconds, data_volume_bytes, start_time, end_time, switch_id, msisdn_hash, imsi_hash (for analytics views), msisdn_raw and imsi_raw encrypted columns (restricted access). hlr_enrichment lookup table with tariff_class, account_type per imsi_hash.
target_output.api_design: No external-facing API. Snowflake SQL: SELECT cdr_id, call_type, duration_seconds, msisdn_hash FROM canonical_cdrs_masked WHERE start_time BETWEEN :from AND :to AND vendor_id = :vendor. Airflow REST API for pipeline health monitoring by data engineering team.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Vendor SFTP RSA-4096 keys in AWS Secrets Manager. Snowflake private link eliminates internet-facing Snowflake endpoint. MSISDN and IMSI SHA-256 hashed with Secrets Manager salt in Snowflake analytics views; raw fields encrypted and restricted to billing role only. GDPR: canonical CDRs deleted after 3 years; S3 raw files archived to Glacier after 90 days. Data subject deletion requests handled by anonymizing msisdn_raw and imsi_raw fields in Snowflake.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Airflow on EKS with Celery executor and 4 workers for SFTP collection DAGs. EMR Serverless Flink jobs submit on-demand per Airflow trigger. MSK 3-broker cluster in private subnet. Snowflake private link in same AWS region. GitHub Actions pipeline deploys Airflow DAGs and EMR Serverless job definitions via CDK. Airflow connection health checked via custom sensor before each SFTP collection DAG run. CDK stack includes MSK cluster parameter group with auto.create.topics.enable=false to prevent rogue topic creation.
target_output.observability: Datadog monitors SFTP collection success rate per vendor, Flink job completion time, Kafka consumer lag on canonical-cdrs topic, Snowflake COPY INTO row counts. Revenue variance alert fires if hourly CDR count drops >20% vs same-hour previous day. Airflow task failures alerted to data engineering Slack channel.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: EMR Serverless Flink: approximately $800/month for 15-minute cycle processing. MSK 3-broker: approximately $600/month. Snowflake compute: approximately $1,200/month for analytics queries. S3 storage: approximately $200/month. Total approximately $2,800/month. Scaling to 6 vendors doubles Flink processing cost to ~$1,600/month.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 -- MVP (10 weeks): Ericsson MSC and Nokia MSS SFTP collectors, CSV and ASN.1 BER parsers, canonical normalization, Snowflake load, basic deduplication. Phase 2 -- Enrichment and Full Vendors (8 weeks): HLR enrichment Flink job, remaining 4 vendor parsers, GDPR analytics views, revenue variance alerting. Phase 3 -- Streaming (future): Real-time Kafka streams from switch vendors replacing SFTP batch polling.
target_output.development_guidelines: Each vendor parser must be tested with 1,000-record sample CDR fixtures covering edge cases (zero-duration calls, missing fields, malformed records). Flink jobs must be tested with embedded mini cluster in unit tests. CDR deduplication key must be documented per vendor format. Snowflake MERGE statements must include an ON CONFLICT action for all mandatory fields.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: pyasn1 performance insufficient for Nokia MSS high-volume ASN.1 BER parsing within 15-minute SLA. Mitigation: benchmark pyasn1 vs asn1crypto in Phase 1; switch to asn1crypto C extension if throughput insufficient. Risk: HLR replica lag causes enrichment joins to return stale subscriber profiles. Mitigation: record hlr_data_lag_seconds in enriched CDR; analytics queries can filter on acceptable freshness. Tradeoff: EMR Serverless vs self-managed Flink cluster -- EMR Serverless eliminates cluster management but cold-start latency of 60-90 seconds adds to 15-minute SLA; warm pool or pre-started jobs may be required.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Dedup key approach: composite key of vendor_id + switch_node_id + cdr_sequence_number covers Ericsson CSV (explicit sequence) and Nokia ASN.1 (CallEventRecord.sequenceNumber); vendors without sequence numbers use MD5(msisdn+start_time+duration+switch_id) as fallback key. GDPR deletion approach: UPDATE canonical_cdrs SET msisdn_raw=NULL, imsi_raw=NULL WHERE msisdn_raw IN (SELECT msisdn FROM deletion_requests); hash values retained for fraud analytics continuity.

metadata.notes: First-pass CDR pipeline at weak quality; directionally correct but sections lack implementation depth on ASN.1 parser, dedup key, and GDPR masking specifics.

profile.domain: 5G NR Site Acceptance Testing Mobile Application

project_goal.value: Mobile app enabling field technicians to conduct 5G NR site acceptance testing with offline-capable RF drive test measurement capture, antenna alignment data recording, and KPI result collection, syncing to a central platform for RF planning engineer sign-off
project_goal.rationale: Paper-based site acceptance is slow and error-prone; mobile app with offline sync eliminates connectivity dependency at remote tower sites

target_users.value: Field technicians conducting drive tests and recording antenna configurations at tower sites; RF planning engineers reviewing and approving site surveys on the backend platform
target_users.rationale: Two-sided workflow: field capture on mobile, engineering review on backend

project_class.rationale: React Native mobile app with offline-first data capture and backend sync; central ECS Fargate backend for review workflow

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, integrations, realtime
capabilities.rationale: React Native frontend, Go backend, PostgreSQL data, Cognito auth, network inventory integration, real-time sync on connectivity restore

complexity_level.rationale: Offline-first sync with conflict resolution is the main technical challenge; core domain logic is straightforward CRUD with approval workflow

risk_level.rationale: Lost or corrupted site acceptance data delays site go-live; no safety risk but commercial impact on network rollout schedule

data_sensitivity.rationale: RF measurement data and antenna configurations are internal operational data; no subscriber PII

external_exposure.rationale: Mobile app users authenticate via Cognito; backend API is ECS Fargate behind ALB with Cognito JWT validation

access_model.value: AWS Cognito user pools; field_technician role (create and edit own surveys, read-only on other sites); rf_engineer role (review all surveys, approve/reject, create remediation tasks); admin role (user management)
access_model.rationale: Role separation ensures technicians cannot approve their own work

feature_scope.value: Site survey session management with offline data capture, GPS-tagged drive test measurement recording, antenna configuration forms (tilt, azimuth, height), KPI result entry and pass/fail against acceptance thresholds, photo attachment with GPS metadata, offline queue with conflict-free sync, RF engineer review and approval workflow, network inventory system integration for site status update, automated go-live flag on approval
feature_scope.rationale: Complete site acceptance lifecycle from field capture to go-live

mvp_scope.value: Site survey creation, drive test measurement capture, antenna config forms, offline sync, RF engineer approval workflow; no network inventory integration in MVP
mvp_scope.rationale: Core capture-review-approve workflow first; inventory integration is Phase 2

security_baseline.value: Cognito JWT validation on all API endpoints; TLS 1.3; React Native Keychain for token storage; no sensitive data in AsyncStorage; GPS coordinates encrypted in transit
security_baseline.rationale: Mobile app on field devices requires secure credential storage; React Native Keychain uses OS keychain APIs

frontend_stack.value: React Native with TypeScript; React Navigation for navigation; WatermelonDB for offline SQLite storage with sync adapter
frontend_stack.rationale: React Native cross-platform iOS and Android; WatermelonDB provides offline-first SQLite with sync primitives

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Gin; REST API for mobile sync and RF engineer review
backend_stack.rationale: Go for high-performance sync endpoint; Gin for routing

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS for central survey data; WatermelonDB SQLite on device for offline storage
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for authoritative backend store; WatermelonDB SQLite provides offline-first sync with lazy loading

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate; RDS PostgreSQL; ALB; CloudFront for static assets; Cognito for auth
hosting_target.rationale: Serverless container deployment eliminates EC2 management; existing AWS infrastructure

privacy_retention_policy.value: Site survey data retained 10 years as network build records; drive test measurements retained 5 years; deleted surveys anonymized (technician_id set to NULL after 90 days)
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Network build documentation retention required for regulatory audit; technician identity anonymized to minimize PII exposure

future_scope.value: Network inventory system integration for automated go-live flag; automated KPI threshold comparison vs acceptance criteria library; team leader dashboard for field team progress tracking
future_scope.rationale: Inventory integration is Phase 2; KPI automation is Phase 3

constraints.value: App must work fully offline for up to 72 hours; sync must complete within 30 seconds on 4G connectivity restore; photo attachments limited to 5MB per survey to control storage costs
constraints.rationale: Remote tower sites may have no connectivity for extended periods; technicians must be able to complete full survey workflow offline

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for ECS Fargate metrics; API error rate and sync latency dashboards; Sentry for React Native crash reporting
observability_baseline.rationale: Mobile crash visibility critical for field device fleet management

execution_preference.value: GitHub Actions for CI; ECS Fargate blue/green deployment; Expo EAS for React Native builds
execution_preference.rationale: Expo EAS managed builds for iOS and Android simplify cert management

project.goal: 5G NR site acceptance mobile app with offline-first capture and RF engineer approval workflow
project.target_users: Field technicians and RF planning engineers
project.timeline: MVP Q2; inventory integration Q4

frontend.auth: Cognito JWT stored in React Native Keychain

security.auth: AWS Cognito user pools with role-based JWT claims

open_questions.conflict_resolution: Define conflict resolution strategy when technician edits offline survey that RF engineer has already commented on

reasoner_reviews.product: The offline-first requirement is the core technical challenge. WatermelonDB with a custom sync adapter is the right choice for React Native offline SQLite. The conflict resolution strategy for simultaneous offline edits is critical -- the Round 2 plan defined last-write-wins which is insufficient for the RF engineer comment scenario. Round 3 must finalize field-data-wins for measurement fields and server-wins for status fields.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The WatermelonDB sync protocol uses pull-then-push with server timestamp comparison. The sync adapter must handle the case where an offline survey has been submitted and approved by the RF engineer while the technician was still offline with local edits -- status field must use server-wins, measurement fields must use field-data-wins with a conflict flag set for RF engineer review.
reasoner_reviews.security: Cognito JWT token refresh on sync is important for 72-hour offline scenarios -- access tokens expire in 1 hour; refresh tokens in WatermelonDB sync adapter must handle 401 responses with automatic token refresh before retry. Ensure refresh token is stored in React Native Keychain, not AsyncStorage.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Round 2 resolved the conflict resolution algorithm. Round 3 must address the photo attachment sync -- 5MB limit per survey combined with 30-second sync window may be insufficient if a technician uploads 10 surveys simultaneously on connectivity restore. Implement progressive photo sync separate from metadata sync to avoid blocking survey availability.

backend.service_design: Go Gin REST API on ECS Fargate: sync endpoint (POST /api/v1/sync -- WatermelonDB compatible pull/push protocol), survey endpoints (CRUD for site_surveys, drive_test_measurements, antenna_configurations), review endpoints (POST /api/v1/surveys/{id}/approve, reject, request-revision), photo upload (presigned S3 URL generation), network inventory webhook (POST to inventory system on survey approval)
backend.api_patterns: WatermelonDB sync protocol: POST /api/v1/sync with {lastPulledAt, changes: {created[], updated[], deleted[]}}; server responds with {changes: {created[], updated[], deleted[]}, timestamp}. Conflict resolution: measurement fields field-data-wins; status fields server-wins
backend.failure_handling: Sync partial failure: server applies idempotent changes with created_at deduplication; returns partial success with failed_ids list; client retries failed records on next sync

security.auth_design: Cognito User Pool with field_technician and rf_engineer groups; JWT claims include custom:role; Gin middleware validates Cognito RS256 JWT on every API request; token refresh handled in React Native sync adapter
security.authorization_model: PostgreSQL RLS: field_technicians SELECT/UPDATE WHERE technician_id = current_user_id; rf_engineers SELECT all surveys, UPDATE status fields only; admin full access
security.secrets_management: RDS credentials in AWS Secrets Manager; ECS Task Definition uses Secrets Manager injection; Cognito client secret in Secrets Manager

data.entities: site_surveys (survey_id UUID PK, site_id, technician_id, status ENUM draft/submitted/approved/rejected/revision_requested, created_at, submitted_at, approved_at, conflict_flag BOOL), drive_test_measurements (measurement_id UUID PK, survey_id FK, latitude FLOAT, longitude FLOAT, sinr_db FLOAT, rsrp_dbm FLOAT, rsrq_db FLOAT, throughput_mbps FLOAT, measured_at TIMESTAMP, sequence_num INT), antenna_configurations (config_id UUID PK, survey_id FK, sector_id, tilt_degrees FLOAT, azimuth_degrees FLOAT, height_m FLOAT, recorded_at), kpi_results (kpi_id UUID PK, survey_id FK, kpi_name, value FLOAT, threshold FLOAT, pass_fail ENUM pass/fail, measured_at), sign_off_records (signoff_id UUID PK, survey_id FK, rf_engineer_id, decision ENUM approved/rejected, comments TEXT, signed_at)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ; drive_test_measurements table partitioned by month; photo attachments in S3 with presigned URL access; WatermelonDB on-device schema mirrors server subset
data.migration_notes: New greenfield schema; no migration from legacy paper process

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate service with 2-4 tasks, ALB with Cognito authorizer; RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ in private subnet; S3 bucket for photo attachments with lifecycle rule; CloudFront for static React web (Phase 2 RF engineer web portal)
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Go unit tests, PostgreSQL integration tests with testcontainers, Docker build, ECR push, ECS blue/green deployment. Expo EAS Build for iOS and Android; EAS Submit for App Store/Play Store release
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS blue/green instant rollback via ALB target group weight; RDS point-in-time recovery; WatermelonDB schema versioning with migration scripts

frontend.state_management: WatermelonDB reactive queries via @nozbe/watermelondb observer pattern; React Query for backend-only calls (approval workflow); Redux for auth state
frontend.ui_modules: SurveyListScreen: offline-first list of site_surveys from WatermelonDB with sync status indicators. DriveTestScreen: GPS map with measurement trail, real-time SINR/RSRP input fields, sequence number tracking. AntennaConfigForm: sector selector with tilt/azimuth/height inputs and camera integration for photo capture. KPIResultsScreen: KPI entry with green/red pass/fail indicators vs acceptance thresholds. SyncStatusBar: connectivity indicator, pending records count, last sync timestamp.
frontend.accessibility_notes: Conflict flag badge on survey card when server-wins resolution occurred; technician prompted to review flagged measurements

ISS-001.title: Photo attachment sync blocks metadata sync on connectivity restore
ISS-001.detail: Round 2 sync implementation syncs metadata and photo attachments in a single sync pass. With 5MB limit and up to 10 queued surveys, simultaneous photo upload blocks survey metadata availability to RF engineers for up to 5 minutes, violating the 30-second sync SLA for metadata.

history.note: Photo sync blocking identified
30-second SLA only defined for metadata; photo sync not separated

ISS-002.title: Conflict flag UX not defined for technician review
ISS-002.detail: When server-wins conflict resolution overwrites a technician field measurement, the conflict_flag is set but no UX flow guides the technician to review what changed, leading to silent data overwrites the technician may not notice.

history.note: conflict_flag exists but no UX review flow defined

focus_issues.title: Photo attachment sync blocks metadata sync
focus_issues.detail: Single-pass sync blocks survey metadata availability behind photo upload, violating 30-second metadata SLA.

focus_issues.title: Conflict flag UX not defined
focus_issues.detail: Server-wins overwrites not surfaced to technician in UX.

latest_recommendations: Separate photo sync from metadata sync: run WatermelonDB metadata sync first (target <30s), then background photo upload via separate S3 presigned URL queue
Add ConflictReviewScreen triggered on app open when surveys have conflict_flag=true; show field-by-field diff of server-applied changes
